jobs.title: Client Relationship Specialist – Investment Banking (FSG Business Development)
jobs.description:
We are seeking a talented and motivated individual to work with the Financial Sponsor Group (FSG) within Global Investment Banking. This person will play an integral role in providing analytical and deal support between Global Investment Banking’s industry and product teams on mergers and acquisitions, public offerings and other financial advisory services we provide to our clients. In addition, the position will require the preparation of client presentations and company research to support new business generation with Baird’s private equity clients. This person will perform duties in support of the senior members of the Financial Sponsor Group and/or GP Solutions (GPS) Team. Activities and duties require the development of an understanding of investment banking (including the sell side mergers & acquisitions process), the private equity industry and a high level of organization, expertise and experience in planning and prioritizing tasks. The ideal candidate must have the ability to work in a team-oriented environment, have excellent verbal and written communication skills, knowledge of financial accounting and the ability to deal well with peak workloads. Finally, the candidate should be comfortable providing additional hours of support to the FSG team and the investment banking department outside of normal business hours.

What You’ll Do​

Learn by doing and be trusted with meaningful work from the jump​

  • Spearhead consistent and strong connectivity between GP Solutions (GPS) and the rest of Global Investment Banking (GIB) in new client development activities

  • Coordinate internal and external secondary advisory business development efforts, working with the GPS team to partner with senior bankers across GIB

    • Organize and maintain data on target clients and new business opportunities

    • Coordinate internal discussions with Financial Sponsor Group bankers, driving efforts to deliver GPS capabilities to Baird’s covered sponsor clients

    • Coordinate internal discussions with senior industry bankers, identifying opportunities to pitch GPS capabilities alongside M&A and other Baird GIB solutions 

    • Track, monitor and summarize ongoing internal and external dialogue with and regarding potential new GPS clients 

    • Track real time M&A pitch activities and spearhead efforts to integrate GPS content into those upcoming meetings, where relevant to do so

    • Maintain and organize origination activity data in Excel and Salesforce 

  • Propel business development activities in support of GPS

    • Coordinate client interaction, including topic-driven client dinners, client entertainment events and regular bi-lateral client market updates 

    • Coordinate efforts to engage with clients at Baird Conferences 

    • Coordinate efforts to participate in secondary market conferences and related events 

    • Prepare background summaries for GPS bankers ahead of client development meetings 

  • Assist GPS bankers in collecting and utilizing research on private equity firms and industry trends via access to external publications and database resources (i.e., Pitchbook, Preqin, CapIQ, etc.)

  • Prepare content for GPS internal meetings, including summaries of the forward meeting calendar, live deal updates and summaries of the new client pipeline 

  • Establish and communicate best practices to enhance efficiency and productivity across GPS workflows

What You’ll Gain​

The kind of environment that turns early talent into lasting careers​

  • Opportunity to work directly with Director/MD level without layers in between

  • Fast paced environment that will enable you to grow as a professional

  • Unique culture that values diverse backgrounds and perspectives while emphasizing teamwork and a strong sense of partnership

  • Opportunity to work with industry leaders - since 2010, Baird's Financial Sponsor group has played an integral role on nearly $130 billion worth of equity and M&A transactions.

What It Takes​

We’re looking for people who want to go far, and go together​

  • Bachelor’s degree that includes at least one Accounting class is required. 2-5 years of previous experience in a financial services or professional services organization is preferred

  • The ideal candidate will possess a strong work ethic, a demonstrated record for excellence and have the ability to function equally well in both a team setting and on a self-directed basis.

  • Must be able to work with internal and external clients, including directly reporting to and communicating with senior banker(s) in other Baird offices on a daily basis via email and telephone.

  • Must be detail-oriented and recognize errors in numbers, spelling, grammar and punctuation; must demonstrate good follow-through skills.

  • Thorough working knowledge of Microsoft Office, with an emphasis on Microsoft Excel and Microsoft PowerPoint is required. Must also be very skilled in the use of various internet information data providers and have demonstrated an ability to undertake research projects.

  • Strong critical thinking, analytical and communication skills required.

Compensation & Benefits: 

  • $75,000 – $100,000 annual salary with bonus potential

  • Compensation and bonus are commensurate with experience, performance and/or GIB profitability

  • In addition to a strong culture that is focused on health and well-being, our organization offers competitive total compensation packages and comprehensive benefits including: protected Friday night and Saturdays, periodic sabbaticals during career, shareholder opportunities, mandatory paid time off (PTO) and robust 401k match and profit-sharing contribution

Note to External Recruiters / Search Firms: Baird does not accept unsolicited resumes and will not compensate for any placement resulting from such submissions. Any unsolicited resumes received will not be treated as valid applications.

Baird is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

Baird is committed to diversity and provides employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, citizenship, national origin, age, disability, military service, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, or any other status protected by law.

companyDetails.description: Learn by doing and be trusted with meaningful work from the jump​

jobs.title: Investment Banking Associate, GP Solutions
jobs.description:
Join a Team Leading Momentum in GP-Led Solutions 

Since launching in late 2022, Baird’s Global GP Solutions Group has quickly set itself apart by delivering outstanding results and expanding its advisory footprint at an exceptional pace. With 15 GP-led transactions closed since 2024 – representing more than $5 billion in total volume – you’ll gain exposure to one of the fastest-growing areas in private markets and work on deals that make an impact. Learn more.  

Complex deals, real ownership, and career momentum. Grow further at Baird. ​ Baird is where investment banking careers gain real traction. Associates take on meaningful responsibility, work closely with senior bankers and clients, and grow in a culture that values performance, collaboration, and long-term success.​ 

What You’ll Do​ 

  • Responsibility that shapes your skill. Work that deepens your impact.​ 

  • Work closely with senior management and will be charged with executing and contributing to the investment banking presence in GP Solutions

  • Provide the support to build a meaningful franchise in the sector, with a focus in Secondary Advisory.

  • Drive the preparation of company valuations, financial models, marketing materials, and client presentations​ 

  • Contribute to business development efforts through research and analysis​ 

  • Work directly with senior bankers and client executives, gaining insight into leadership-level decision-making​ 

  • Use AI tools like Rogo and auxi to streamline the creation of pitch books and models, and employ data visualization software like Power BI to generate data-driven insights and reports for client materials 

What You’ll Gain​ 

  • A team that runs on trust, momentum, and shared values​ 

  • Build strong relationships with client leadership and C-suite executives through active involvement in complex transactions​ 

  • Take on greater ownership through lean deal teams that offer more responsibility ​ 

  • Navigate clear paths for progression with mentorship, visibility, and real opportunity​ 

  • Advance alongside elite, high-performing peers who hold each other to a high standard​ 

  • Collaborate across Baird’s global investment banking platform—500+ banking associates working together to drive results across geographies, sectors, and specialties​ 

  • Grow in a culture that values diverse perspectives, welcomes ambition, and builds success through collaboration and shared purpose​ 

​What It Takes​ 

  • We’re looking for people ready to lead with focus and integrity​ 

  • Candidates must have at least three years of experience working on a team of investment bankers with a focus on GP Solutions

  • Deep knowledge of Secondary Advisory and demonstrated ability to effectively execute complex transactions in Secondary Advisory

  • Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and a track record of delivering results​ 

  • Clear communication, strong leadership, and the ability to thrive in a collaborative, high-performing team​ 

  • Intellectual agility, initiative, and the drive to grow in a fast-paced, client-focused environment​ 

  • Experience with AI tools such as Rogo, CoPilot, and auxi is preferred 

  • Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar 

Compensation and Benefits: 

  • $175,000 - $200,000 annual salary with bonus potential 

  • Compensation and bonus are commensurate with experience, performance and/or GIB profitability 

In addition to a strong culture that is focused on health and well-being, our organization offers competitive total compensation packages and comprehensive benefits including: protected Friday night and Saturdays, periodic sabbaticals during career, shareholder opportunities, mandatory paid time off (PTO) and robust 401k match and profit-sharing contribution 

Note to External Recruiters / Search Firms: Baird does not accept unsolicited resumes and will not compensate for any placement resulting from such submissions. Any unsolicited resumes received will not be treated as valid applications.  

Baird is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status. 

Baird is committed to diversity and provides employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, citizenship, national origin, age, disability, military service, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, or any other status protected by law.

jobs.title: Client Portfolio Manager
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.
Davidson Investment Advisors is seeking a Client Portfolio Manager to join our growing team. This role is an exciting opportunity to help serve and expand a key segment of our business while contributing to the strong culture of our firm. 
Summary: 
The Client Portfolio Manager plays a critical role in serving nonprofit, endowment, corporate, insurance, and charitable trust clients by helping them structure, manage, and steward their investment portfolios. This position offers the opportunity to work closely with our business development, investment, and operations teams to deliver exceptional client experiences rooted in integrity, professionalism, and a deep understanding of financial markets.
Ideal candidates bring strong communication skills, a passion for mission aligned investing, and the ability to build trust with complex institutional clients. While this role is primarily client service focused, it also plays an important part in driving the ongoing growth and refinement of this business segment.
 Qualifications/Requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree in finance, business or a related field
• 5+ years of experience in financial services, insurance, banking, or nonprofit leadership
• Ability to work independently, analyze complex problems, and provide thoughtful, solutions-oriented recommendations
• Strong understanding of investment strategies and best practices for institutional and nonprofit clients
• Excellent organization and proficiency with Client Relationship Management systems
• High proficiency in Microsoft Office 
• Self-starter with excellent organizational and communication skills, with the ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment effectively
• Detail-oriented with a strong sense of ownership and accountability 
• Collaborative mindset with a commitment to contributing to team success.
Duties: 
• Provide exceptional client service to institutional clients of the firm
o Prepare and present to boards of directors and other decision makers
o Maintain consistent communication with staff and board members regarding market conditions, portfolio strategies, performance, and potential enhancements
o Collaborate with investment and operations teams to align investment strategies and operational deliverables with client needs
o Monitor client portfolios to ensure ongoing compliance with Investment Policies 
• Implement investment strategies for clients that align with their objectives and risk tolerances
• Prospect, pitch, and convert new institutional clients to our firm’s services
• Work closely with D.A. Davidson Financial Advisors to deliver integrated solutions for shared clients
• Drive initiatives to enhance efficiency and optimize workflows; proactively seek ways to improve the team’s processes
• Perform any other duties and special projects as necessary Adhere to all the firm’s Code of Ethics and Policies and Procedures, as well as its Team Agreement, which is a commitment to the following behaviors:
o Curiosity (avoid becoming defensive and remaining open to learning)
o Accountability (taking 100% responsibility (no more; no less) for results; no blaming)
o Candor (engaging in courageous truth-telling to increase power and speed in decisions)
o Awareness (tapping every emotional and intuitional resource for decisions)
o Passion (discovering and allowing people to do what they do best)
o Appreciation (expressing gratitude and fostering a culture of sincere cooperation)
What we offer:
Competitive salary plus excellent benefits and perks including, but not limited to:  
• Medical, Dental and Vision
• Company 401(k)and ESOP contribution 
• Generous sick, vacation, and maternity/parental leave
• Paid holidays
• Professional Development Opportunities
• Tuition Reimbursement ($15,000 lifetime cap)
• Discounted personal insurance including home, auto and recreational vehicles
• Charitable gift-matching program
• Davidson Day of Giving – Our tradition of positively impacting communities in which we live and work.  
The compensation offered will be determined on a case-by-case basis considering a variety of factors including, but not limited to, the skills, relevant work experience, and geographic location of each specific candidate.

jobs.title: Equity Capital Markets – Vice President
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson & Co. - the Strength of Advice
Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.

D.A. Davidson & Co., a leading investment banking firm, is accepting applicants for a full-time Vice President position in our Equity Capital Markets group based in New York City. The Vice President is expected to project manage strategic assignments assisting clients with public offerings, private placements and other advisory services. The Vice President is expected to translate Director and Managing Director wishes into achievable tasks, assign projects to Associates and Analysts and see these projects through to completion. The Vice President is expected to be a coach to junior bankers and build relationships with clients.  

Qualifications:

  • Strong transaction history – Capital Markets experience preferred
  • Ability to lead transaction execution teams.
  • High degree of comfort interacting with senior executives.
  • Strong financial modeling ability and Microsoft Office expertise.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Superior work ethic and teamwork skills.
  • Good judgment and ability to exercise discretion when handling confidential information.
  • Ability to perform in both team and self-directed settings.
  • MBA with at least 3 years of capital markets experience or undergraduate degree with a minimum of 6 years capital markets experience.
  • Possession of Series 79 (Series 7 may be applicable) and 63 licenses preferred but not required. New hires without these licenses will be required upon hiring.

In this role you will:

  • Lead the day-to-day execution of transactions (Public Offerings / Private Placements), including preparing and reviewing company marketing documents and reviewing financial models, managing marketing and coordinating due diligence, analyzing financing alternatives and negotiating documentation.
  • Establish client trust by identifying needs, project leadership, transaction structuring, company positioning, due diligence, negotiating, etc.
  • Assist senior bankers in the business development efforts.
  • Contribute to revenue targets for deal teams through relationship development and identifying deal opportunities for the firm.
  • Manage, train and develop Associates and Analysts on the team.
  • Ensure quality collaboration and production of work flow and product from Associates and Analysts.

jobs.title: Equity Research Associate - Tech and Diversified Industrials Groups - Class of 2026
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson & Co. - the Strength of Advice
Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.
D.A. Davidson is accepting applications for an Equity Research Associate position in it's Portland, OR  or New York offices. Research associates partner with Analysts to provide differentiated analysis and value-added insight regarding publicly traded companies and industry trends for institutional clients. Associates are part of small teams with a high level of responsibility in contributing to published research. Associates become experts in financial modeling and proprietary research techniques.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree with a minimum 3.5 GPA
  • Background in accounting and finance strongly preferred
  • Bright, mature, and motivated with the desire to excel and ability to adapt in an intellectually challenging environment
  • Passionate about capital markets and a desire to become highly skilled in equity research
  • Strong analytical and organizational skills
  • Ability to collaborate effectively within a team and work well on a self-directed basis
  • Working knowledge of MS Office, specifically Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
  • Proficient communication and presentation skills
  • Currently hold or be open to obtaining the following licenses: Securities Industry Essentials (SIE), Series 63, Series 86 and Series 87.

Duties:

  • Support Research Analyst(s) in:
  • Making bold calls and produce actionable research for Davidson’s institutional clients
  • Researching market and industry trends and company specifics to become an expert in your sector
  • Creating, maintaining, and updating data in financial models, and creating tables and graphs that clearly illustrate the information
  • Gain experience necessary to:
  • Formulate and issue opinions and recommendations on securities and companies in one or more industry groups
  • Prepare reports and statistical data for dissemination to firm clients and the investing public
  • Answer inquiries from customers, personnel of the firm, and the general public regarding individual securities or industry groups
  • Discuss research findings with Sales & Trading associates and institutional clients (e.g., analysts and portfolio managers at top mutual funds and hedge funds)

D.A. Davidson & Co. - the Strength of Advice
Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Investment Banking – Experienced Analyst
D.A. Davidson & Co., a leading full-service investment banking firm with operations nationwide, is accepting applicants for full-time Analyst positions working with the firm’s Investment Banking Diversified Industrials Group in our Chicago, IL office. The Experienced Analyst will play an important role working with senior bankers on strategic assignments, assisting clients with mergers and acquisitions, public offerings, private placements and other advisory services. 

Qualifications:

  • Bright, professional, and motivated with the desire to excel in an intellectually challenging environment
  • 1-3 years of experience working in investment banking or transaction experience in related fields such as accounting, consulting or corporate finance required.
  • Interest and curiosity in the Diversified Industrials sector
  • Bachelor’s degree with a minimum 3.5 GPA.
  • Working knowledge of business software (especially Word, Excel and PowerPoint).
  • High level of financial knowledge (financial modeling, macroeconomics, current events, etc.).
  • Excellent verbal and written communications skills, strong work ethic and teamwork skills. Self-motivated, with a strong desire to excel in a challenging field.

  • Flexibility to work long hours, nights, and weekends, as needed for workflow.
  • Possession of Securities Industries Essentials (SIE), Series 79, and 63 licenses preferred but not required. New hires without these licenses will require successful completion upon hiring.

  • Develop financial modules to analyze a wide variety of client-specific projects.

  • Support business development activities of senior investment bankers.
  • Attend client meetings and interact on transactional and technical issues.
  • Actively participate in live deals, such as M&A, IPOs, and advisory.

jobs.title: Managed Assets Research Associate
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.
Summary/Function: 
D.A. Davidson & Co.’s Managed Assets Research Department provides a multitude of services for our firm’s advisors and their clients. The research team is responsible for developing and maintaining recommendations across the mutual fund, ETF and Separately Managed Account (SMA) landscape. Further, this research department is also responsible for managing a wide array of asset allocation portfolios on a discretionary basis for our firm’s advisors and their clients. Communications with our financial advisors are primarily provided through quarterly presentations, model portfolio reports, client portfolio reviews, conference calls, and availability by direct phone or email for engagement with our advisors. 
The primary function of the Research Associate position is to support the overall activities of the Managed Assets Research department. We are looking for an associate with an organic interest in the financial markets and investments, who is motivated by the prospect of playing a vital role in delivering high level of service and quality investment solutions to our advisors and their clients. 
Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in economics, business, marketing or related field required. 
• Series 7 & 65/66, or willingness to obtain.
• Progress toward, or desire to pursue, the CFA or CAIA charter designation also viewed positively.
• 2+ years of experience in the financial services industry, preferably in an environment of working with financial advisors.
• Strong writing and proofreading skills.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel, Word and PowerPoint. Experience with Adobe Acrobat, and/or Morningstar Direct a significant plus.
• Strong communication skills, written and verbal, with excellent organizational skills.
• Articulate, confident, driven professional with the ability to work well independently, and as part of a cohesive team in a fast-paced environment. 
Duties:
• Provide research and marketing support, as needed, to the department’s director and broader team.
• Assist in the continued development of the research team’s communications process and procedures.  This consists of, but is not limited to:
o Proofreading/editing text, creating supporting charts & tables, and ultimately distributing presentations and reports through different mediums.
o Maintaining and developing investment reports and note templates.
o Maintain and continue to develop the Managed Assets Research intranet site, internal department databases, and other communications. 
o Keeping organized files for our internal and external publications and other department activities, as appropriate.
• Respond to frequent ad hoc research requests from financial advisors, typically by phone and/or email.
• Assist the research team in client portfolio reviews and proposals. Recommendations in these reviews and proposals often utilize the team’s asset allocation portfolios and selections from our Supervised and Approved Research lists for Mutual Funds, ETF’s and SMA’s. 
What we offer:
Competitive salary plus excellent benefits and perks including, but not limited to:  
•Medical, Dental and Vision
•Company 401(k)and ESOP contribution 
•Generous sick, vacation, and maternity/parental leave
•Paid holidays
•Professional Development Opportunities
•Tuition Reimbursement ($15,000 lifetime cap)
•Discounted personal insurance including home, auto and recreational vehicles
•Charitable gift-matching program
•Davidson Day of Giving – Our tradition of positively impacting communities in which we live and work.  
The potential base pay hiring range for this role is $90,000 – 110,000 annually. The compensation offered will be determined on a case-by-case basis considering a variety of factors including, but not limited to, the skills, relevant work experience, and geographic location of each specific candidate.
D.A. Davidson has been in business for over 90 years! As an employee-owned company, our success is driven by our high standards of business ethics, integrity and the belief that our Associates are our most important assets.  We hope you will consider joining us!

jobs.title: Quantitative Analyst, Public Finance
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.

Summary/Function: 

The Quantitative Analyst supports Fixed Income Capital Markets by providing financial modeling, analytical support, pricing, and quantitative insights within the Development Finance Group for public finance and structured transactions. This role is hands-on, production-oriented, and designed for an individual capable of operating as a senior contributor within the quant group and investment banking team.  This role is designed for individuals with modeling skills for debt transactions who is ready to work with the Development Finance Team on financings across the nation. 

The position partners closely with bankers, traders, and senior leadership to support deal execution, debt service analysis, structuring, and client deliverables.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, or a related field
  • 3+ years of experience in public finance, fixed income, structured products, or quantitative financial analysis
  • Demonstrated ability to function as a senior or near senior quant without extensive ramp up
  • Strong understanding of municipal bond structures, debt service mechanics, and pricing conventions
  • Advanced proficiency in Excel (complex formulas, scenarios, DBC experience is a plus)
  • Experience with financial modeling software, databases, or coding is beneficial
  • Ability to manage multiple live priorities in deadline-driven environments
  • Strong analytical and quantitative reasoning skills
  • High attention to detail, accuracy, and internal quality control
  • Ability to communicate complex analyses clearly to non-quant stakeholders
  • Sound judgment under pressure
  • Collaborative mindset with strong service orientation to bankers and clients
  • Professional discretion and reliability
  • Experience supporting development finance, housing, public finance, or specialty finance sectors
  • Interest in growing into broader responsibilities over time

Duties:

  • Build, audit, and maintain cash flows for debt models, pricing models, and sensitivity analysis for transactions
  • Support new-issue pricing, refunding analyses, and scenario modeling across client deals
  • Assist bankers with structuring solutions, yield calculations, debt service schedules, and alternative financing comparisons
  • Provide quantitative support during live transactions, including rapid-turn analyses under tight deadlines
  • Incorporate feedback from traders and bankers to evaluate market conditions, investor demand, and relative value
  • Review, analyze, and validate assumptions used in offering materials and client presentations
  • Support ongoing internal tools, templates, and model enhancements to improve efficiency and consistency
  • Collaborate across quant and banking teams to balance workload during peak periods
  • Ensure analytical work is accurate and meets internal and regulatory expectations

Why this position:

  • High-visibility seat within Fixed Income Capital Markets with a growing and dynamic team specializing in Development Finance nationwide.
  • Direct exposure to live transactions, senior banking, and senior leadership
  • Opportunity to make immediate impact during a period of strong deal flow and demand

jobs.title: Senior Associate/Associate, Portfolio Management, Product and Investor Relations, Real Estate
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

We are looking to hire a Senior Associate/Associate to join our Real Estate Portfolio Management, Product and Investor Relations team in Hong Kong.

You will be working closely with Portfolio Management and Product & Investor Relations colleagues across the full lifecycle of fund management and capital raising activities. The role offers broad exposure to investment strategy, portfolio analysis, investor engagement, and new business initiatives, with responsibility for supporting ongoing fund operations, live capital raises, and prospective client and investor engagement across the platform.

Primary Functions and Essential Responsibilities

  • Support the Portfolio Management and Product & Investor Relations (IR) teams

  • Portfolio Management centered around strategy, investment review, portfolio analysis, internal and external reporting

    • Create and update asset and fund-level financial models

    • Perform in-depth portfolio analysis and reports

    • Conduct in-depth macroeconomic and sector research

    • New business development initiatives across all stages

  • Product and IR duties centered around the execution of live capital raises

    • Prepare marketing and investor due diligence materials (Investor Presentation, INREV DDQ, Private Placement Memorandum, etc.)

    • Assist investors with their queries and investment/operational/ESG due diligence questionnaires; organize and attend regular due diligence meetings and asset tours

    • Liaise and coordinate with third-party legal/tax/ESG consultants and advisors

    • Work with different teams and offices to review transaction documentation, negotiate key terms of the transaction with prospective investors, and close transactions

    • Support senior management with prospective client/investor meeting, including the preparation of investor profile, financial analysis, market assessment, recent transactions report, etc.); participate in prospective client/investor meetings

    • Maintain deal and investor profiles on our CRM system

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline

  • Prior experience in investment banking, private equity, and/or management consulting

  • Exceptional junior candidates at Analyst level, including recent graduates, may also be considered

  • Highly proficient with Microsoft Office (i.e., Word, Excel, PowerPoint) with excellent numeracy, analytical, presentation, and organizational skills

  • Self‑starter with a proactive mindset, able to think ahead, consistently meet deadlines, and anticipate next steps

  • Capacity to multitask, work independently across multiple projects and thrive in a team

  • Rigorous attention to detail and problem-solving skills

  • Intellectual curiosity and improvement mindset

  • Academic excellence, strong written and verbal communication skills, excellent interpersonal and relationship-building skills with colleagues and clients at all levels

  • High standards of professional and ethical conduct

  • Fluent in English and Japanese (spoken and written) is required (JLPT N1 or equivalent proficiency)

Reporting Relationships

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Analyst, Commercial Finance, Collateral Analyst
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

Ares Management, L.P. (“Ares”) is an industry-leading provider of asset-backed loans to middle-market businesses located in the United States and Canada. The Ares Commercial Finance platform (“ACF”) provides asset-based and cash flow loans to small and middle-market companies, as well as asset-backed facilities to specialty finance companies. ACF’s asset-based lines of credit may be structured as working capital and bridge financing, special accommodation financing, turnaround financing, debtor-in-possession financing, and acquisition financing. ACF’s asset-based lending practice focuses on privately held and small public companies in the middle market and addresses a wide breadth of industries including manufacturing, distribution, wholesale, and service companies.

The candidate will be provided with an introduction to asset-based lending principles beginning with collateral monitoring, including understanding a borrowing base formula, understanding, identifying, and tracking collateral trends, collateral reconciliation and verification; relationship management, including daily correspondence with borrowers, team members, and senior management; and an introduction to credit principles. The Collateral Analyst will be expected to identify emerging risks, understand how collateral performance impacts credit exposure, and exercise sound judgment in escalating issues within a controlled lending environment.

The collateral analyst’s primary role is to ensure the accuracy, integrity, and ongoing monitoring of loan collateral, enabling informed credit decisions, reliable funding availability, and early identification of collateral‑driven risk.

Primary Functions and Essential Responsibilities

  • Review borrower’s collateral reporting package for accuracy, trends, and adherence to lender guidelines on a portfolio of secured revolving credit loans
  • Coordinating borrowing base availability changes with treasury or cash management teams
  • Ensure timely and accurate posting of daily cash receipts to loan balances
  • Calculate and monitor customer’s daily borrowing capacity and process funding requests
  • Post collateral values to our proprietary loan monitoring platform (currently Stucky)
  • Prepare month-end collateral trend analyses (the monthly Trend Card), reconciliations of loan and collateral balances and verifying monthly interest and fees are correctly processed
  • Maintain and monitor tranche‑level terms, including effective dates, expiration dates, interest, and fee calculations
  • Perform monthly accounts receivable verifications via telephone, email, portals or cash tracing
  • Maintain a monthly Client Checklist to track borrower reporting and loan maintenance exceptions
  • Supporting internal, external, and regulatory audits by assembling collateral and availability documentation
  • Follow up and review of field exams, including the preparation of the standard field exam review form.
  • Follow up and review of appraisals for inventory and fixed assets and ensuring that the values are posted to our proprietary loan monitoring platform (Stucky).
  • Follow up for borrower compliance with insurance requirement
  • Monitoring and reviewing regularly scheduled lien and tax searches
  • Involvement with the new business deal team in setting up the borrower’s initial borrowing base certificate and preparing the operations Handling Guide.
  • Assist Credit Officers with daily portfolio management, risk assessment, and due diligence
  • Escalating collateral concerns or emerging risks to portfolio management or senior credit teams
  • On-going communication with fellow team members, customers, their customers, and Ares senior management
  • Perform other duties as assigned

Qualifications

  • Education: Qualified candidates will have a college degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business Administration or a minimum of two years’ experience in the Asset Based Lending industry
  • ExperienceRequired:Proficiency in Excel, Word andPowerPoint

GeneralRequirements:

  • Outgoingpersonalitywithastrongdesiretobeacontributingteammember
  • Excellentoralandwrittencommunicationskills
  • Strongmathematicaptitudewithaninterestinfinanceandaccounting
  • Intellectual curiosity and willingness to learn
  • Abilitytofocusonroutinetasksinafast-paced,team-orientedworkenvironment
  • Abilitytomaintainconfidentiality
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and spreadsheets, including reconciliations, trend analysis, and data validation
  • Strong organizational and time‑management skills, enabling them to meet daily, weekly, and month‑end reporting deadlines
  • Ability to identify exceptions, inconsistencies, and anomalies in collateral reporting and escalate appropriately

Reporting Relationships

Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this position is listed below. Total compensation may also include a discretionary performance-based bonus. Note, the range takes into account a broad spectrum of qualifications, including, but not limited to, years of relevant work experience, education, and other relevant qualifications specific to the role.

$80,000.00 - $90,000.00

The firm also offers robust Benefits offerings. Ares U.S. Core Benefits include Comprehensive Medical/Rx, Dental and Vision plans; 401(k) program with company match; Flexible Savings Accounts (FSA); Healthcare Savings Accounts (HSA) with company contribution; Basic and Voluntary Life Insurance; Long-Term Disability (LTD) and Short-Term Disability (STD) insurance; Employee Assistance Program (EAP), and Commuter Benefits plan for parking and transit.

Ares offers a number of additional benefits including access to a world-class medical advisory team, a mental health app that includes coaching, therapy and psychiatry, a mindfulness and wellbeing app, financial wellness benefit that includes access to a financial advisor, new parent leave, reproductive and adoption assistance, emergency backup care, matching gift program, education sponsorship program, and much more.

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Analyst, US Direct Lending (Ivy Hill)
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

Ivy Hill Asset Management (“IHAM”), an SEC-registered investment adviser and wholly owned portfolio company of Ares Capital Corporation (“ARCC”), primarily focuses on investing in senior secured debt in middle market companies. As of December 31, 2025, IHAM managed approximately $14.6 billion in AUM through structured investment vehicles and managed accounts.

IHAM is seeking a highly motivated, detail-oriented Analyst to join its Investment team. This execution-focused role is designed for candidates early in their careers who are eager to develop deep technical, analytical, and credit skills within an entrepreneurial, high-performance environment. Over time, strong performers will gain broader exposure, increasing complexity in their work, and a clear path toward greater responsibility as they progress within the firm.

Key Position Responsibilities:

The Analyst will play a critical support role and work with junior & senior investment professionals across the full investment lifecycle, including underwriting & credit analysis of new financing opportunities, post-closing portfolio management, fundraising, and broader firm strategic initiatives.

Investments & Underwriting

  • Assist the deal team in screening new investment opportunities and re-underwriting existing credits
  • Perform complex financial analysis, market research, and LBO modeling, including base case & downside sensitivities
  • Support Associates/Senior Associates in preparing Investment Committee materials
  • Assist in coordinating deal closings with internal teams and external counterparties
  • Gain exposure as a generalist across multiple industry verticals

Portfolio Management

  • Support senior investment professionals in monitoring a portfolio of existing investments
  • Review borrower financials and covenant compliance, and prepare internal portfolio reporting and quarterly valuations
  • Assist with analytical support for amendment processes, secondary market purchases, and workout or restructuring situations

Fund Management & Strategic

  • Assist fund managers in monitoring deployment and performance of existing structured vehicles and managed accounts on a monthly and quarterly basis
  • Maintain complex, Excel-based CLO models for quarterly fund valuations
  • Support new fundraising efforts, including preparation of loan tapes, portfolio analysis, investor due diligence questionnaires, and CLO models
  • Assist with the preparation of quarterly industry reports, IHAM marketing materials, and cash analysis

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree (or international equivalent), preferably in Finance, Accounting, Economics or a related field with an outstanding academic record
  • Relevant internship and/or up to 1-2 years of experience in private credit, leveraged finance, investment banking, or buyside research
  • Strong analytical, financial modeling, and research capabilities
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and PowerPoint skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Detail-oriented, self-motivated, and able to manage multiple workstreams in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
  • Impeccable integrity, intellectual curiosity, and a strong desire to learn

Reporting Relationships

Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this position is listed below. Total compensation may also include a discretionary performance-based bonus. Note, the range takes into account a broad spectrum of qualifications, including, but not limited to, years of relevant work experience, education, and other relevant qualifications specific to the role.

$120,000.00 - $130,000.00

The firm also offers robust Benefits offerings. Ares U.S. Core Benefits include Comprehensive Medical/Rx, Dental and Vision plans; 401(k) program with company match; Flexible Savings Accounts (FSA); Healthcare Savings Accounts (HSA) with company contribution; Basic and Voluntary Life Insurance; Long-Term Disability (LTD) and Short-Term Disability (STD) insurance; Employee Assistance Program (EAP), and Commuter Benefits plan for parking and transit.

Ares offers a number of additional benefits including access to a world-class medical advisory team, a mental health app that includes coaching, therapy and psychiatry, a mindfulness and wellbeing app, financial wellness benefit that includes access to a financial advisor, new parent leave, reproductive and adoption assistance, emergency backup care, matching gift program, education sponsorship program, and much more.

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Head of Southeast Asia & Hong Kong, APAC Relationship Management
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

The Managing Director, Head of Southeast Asia & Hong Kong ("Coverage Market"), APAC Relationship Management, is responsible for leading Ares’ client coverage, fundraising strategy, and long‑term relationship development across key Coverage Markets, with a particular focus on institutional investors including sovereign wealth funds, insurers, and pension systems. This role serves as a senior commercial leader for this Coverage Market, accountable for driving capital raising across the Ares platform, expanding strategic relationships, and representing Ares with the firm’s most significant and complex investors. The role includes leadership responsibility for regional Relationship Management professionals and close partnership with global investment, product, legal, compliance, and operations teams. The position operates with a high degree of autonomy and strategic influence and is a key contributor to Ares’ global fundraising objectives. The role reports to the head of APAC Relationship Management.

Primary Functions and Essential Responsibilities

Client Coverage & Fundraising

  • Lead institutional client coverage and fundraising activities across Southeast Asia, & Hong Kong, with responsibility for significant AUM growth across credit and alternative strategies

  • Originate, develop, and maintain senior‑level relationships with large institutional investors, including sovereign wealth funds, insurers, and pensions

  • Drive both new investor acquisition and expansion of existing client relationships across multiple strategies and mandates

  • Serve as a senior commercial representative of Ares in the region, engaging with clients on complex investment structures, bespoke mandates, and long‑term partnerships

Strategic Leadership

  • Shape and execute Ares’ APAC fundraising strategy in partnership with Global Client Solutions leadership

  • Identify regional market trends and growth opportunities to inform product strategy, investor targeting, and resource allocation

  • Act as a regional thought partner to investment teams on investor positioning, product development, and market demand

Team Leadership & Talent Development

  • Lead, manage, and develop a regional Relationship Management team, including hiring, mentoring, and performance management

  • Create a high‑performing, collaborative team culture aligned with Ares’ CREST values

  • Provide coaching and guidance to junior and mid‑level team members to build long‑term organizational capability

Cross‑Functional Collaboration

  • Partner closely with global Relationship Management, Product, Investment, Legal, Compliance, and Operations teams to deliver coordinated client solutions

  • Navigate internal stakeholder relationships to align firm priorities and deliver high‑quality client outcomes

  • Ensure consistency in client messaging, governance, and execution across regions and strategies

Risk Management & Governance

  • Exercise sound judgment in balancing growth opportunities with reputational, regulatory, and operational risk considerations

  • Ensure client engagements and fundraising activities adhere to internal governance standards and regulatory requirements

  • Serve as a senior escalation point for complex client or transactional matters in the region

Leadership & Influence

  • Acts as a visible culture carrier and senior leader for Ares in the APAC region

  • Represents the firm in high‑stakes client discussions, negotiations, and strategic forums

  • Operates collaboratively on matters of significant commercial and strategic importance while remaining aligned with firmwide objectives

Qualifications

  • Minimum of 15 years of experience in institutional relationship management, fundraising, or investor coverage within alternatives, credit, or asset management

  • Deep understanding of institutional investor and regional investment dynamics in Coverage Markets

  • Proven track record of driving significant capital formation and long‑term client growth

  • Demonstrated ability to lead, mentor, and scale high‑performing teams

  • Strong judgment, executive presence, and stakeholder management skills

Reporting Relationships

Partner, Head of APAC Relationship Management

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Associate, Product Management and Investor Relations, Credit
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

Ares is currently searching for a highly motivated, detail-oriented team player to join as an Associate in the European Credit Group specializing in Product Management and Investor Relations in the London office. This individual will be working closely with the London-based Product Management and Investor Relations group to provide investor relations support, including fundraising, ongoing fund reporting for investors, investor presentation support, addressing investor information requests and market/competitor research analysis. He/she must have strong organizational, interpersonal, quantitative and analytical skills and must be able to meet deadline requirements.

PRIMARY FUNCTIONS AND ESSENTIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
The Associate will work in the European Credit group and support Product Specialists in providing information and expertise on firm strategies and funds to clients. The Associate will assist in producing fund reporting for investors on a monthly, quarterly and ad hoc basis, as well as assisting in building new client materials and various prospect/marketing materials, as well as updating market and fund specific data within existing materials.

  • Interface and develop strong working relationships with all internal subject matter experts of the Firm (primarily Portfolio Management and Finance, as well as Operations, Legal, Compliance and Human Resources)
  • Produce monthly/quarterly portfolio reports for investors in commingled funds and separately managed accounts, working with Finance and Portfolio Management teams to collate relevant data
  • Liaise with Finance, Portfolio Management, Legal and Compliance teams to respond to a broad range of investor requests including audit confirmations, financial statement and fee data requests, as well as processing updates to investor details such as wire instructions and authorized signatories
  • Drive process for creating responses to investor questionnaires, due diligence, and ad hoc requests for existing and potential clients
  • Create and edit marketing investor materials for co-investment and funds
  • Maintain quantitative and qualitative key metrics on competitors
  • Build and “own” client materials and various prospect/marketing materials, updating market and fund specific data within existing materials on a quarterly or ad hoc basis
  • Take on ad hoc projects and support all other teammates with their work as needed

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Minimum of 2-3 years of experience
  • Bachelor's degree with strong academic performance
  • Fixed income or loan/credit experience preferred but not required
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills, ability to communicate effectively with all levels of the organization and represent the Firm in a professional manner
  • Exceptional attention to detail, placing a high priority on accuracy and organization
  • Highly proficient in Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Problem solver with ability to research solutions and suggest resolutions
  • Highly motivated self-starter with the ability to set priorities, multi-task and monitor own workload to meet aggressive deadlines
  • Takes initiative and has a strong work ethic
  • Creative, ready to think outside the box and add input to improving existing workflows
  • Proficiency in financial accounting and quantitative analysis skills
  • Demonstrated experience working in a team environment with multiple tasks

Reporting Relationships

Managing Director, Credit IR

There is no set deadline to apply for this job opportunity. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the search is no longer active.

jobs.title: Senior Associate / Vice President, Real Estate Equity Asset Management
jobs.description:
Over the last 20 years, Ares’ success has been driven by our people and our culture. Today, our team is guided by our core values – Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy – and our purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity and a better future. Through our recruitment, career development and employee-focused programming, we are committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive work environment where high-performance talent of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives can build careers within this exciting and growing industry.

Job Description

We are seeking a Senior Associate or Vice President to join our Residential / Living Asset Management team based in London. This individual will be responsible for driving value creation, operational performance, and disciplined execution across Ares’ Residential and Living portfolio across EMEA, including multifamily, student housing and other living strategies as applicable.

Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Develop and execute strategic asset management plans for a diverse portfolio of real estate investments across the living sectors in Europe. Focus will be on pan-European operational real estate, including involvement in backed management companies
  • Coordinate debt workstreams, including refinancing of assets and any restructuring
  • Lead conversations with JV partners to drive forward business plans
  • Manage the disposal process, including leading all transaction execution workstreams
  • Monitor and evaluate property performance, and market trends to identify opportunities for value enhancement and cash flow optimisation.
  • Active review of any property management agreements / lease agreements / franchise contracts to ensure optimal strategy for assets / portfolios
  • Implement and oversee property-level business plans including capital expenditure programs and operational strategies to maximize asset value and investor returns.
  • Oversee quarterly business plan and valuation updates
  • Assist in driving the multifamily asset management strategy across the business
  • Collaborate closely with internal teams, including acquisitions, finance, legal and investor relations

  • Experience working in operational real estate asset classes
  • Experience working on bank refinancings and debt restructurings
  • Outstanding academic record from a top-tier university with a minimum 2:1 (or equivalent)
  • Analytical and technical-minded, able to conduct operational analysis, with strong financial modelling skills required
  • Hands-on, with the ability to be effective in a small and nimble team
  • Excellent attention to detail

Reporting Relationships

Managing Director, Real Estate Equity Asset Management

jobs.title: Deal Team Analyst 2026
jobs.description:
As a Bengaluru Deal Team Analyst, you will work directly with deal teams in EMEA market, supporting the investment process from origination to execution. The role offers a significant opportunity to kick-start your career in Private Equity, with potential career progression to Associate roles in major hubs globally with EQT.

Key Responsibilities:
  • Staffed on the end-to-end process of deals collaborating with global investment teams where you will be an active day-to-day member of the team.
  • Assist in Deal Origination tasks including market mapping, competitor analyses, and company profiling.
  • Engage in Deal Assessment by conducting industry deep-dives, peer benchmarking, and sizing Total Addressable Market (TAM) / Serviceable Available Market (SAM).
  • Perform financial analysis and develop valuation models.
  • Prepare presentations and reports for investment teams (i.e. investment committee and portfolio performance materials) and relevant external stakeholders e.g. advising banks.
  • Actively participate in the transaction process, contributing to project management and investment strategy.
  • Support Portfolio Monitoring through KPI tracking, portfolio benchmarking, and aiding further M&A activities.
  • Participate in the Analyst Program training and provide onward training and mentoring to later intakes.
Basic Qualifications:
  • MBA / Master’s degree in Economics, Finance, Accounting, or a related field.
  • Minimum 1 year of relevant deal experience in Investment Banking or related financial services.
  • Strong analytical skills and a keen interest in finance and valuation principles.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work collaboratively across global teams.
Ideal Candidates:
  • Proactive, enthusiastic, and team-oriented approach.
  • Strong client service orientation and commitment to meeting tight deadlines.
  • Ability to handle multiple workstreams simultaneously in a fast-paced environment
  • Detail-oriented with an analytical mindset.
  • A passion for diversity, equity, and inclusion.
What We Offer:
  • Exposure and a broad range of responsibilities offering a robust start to your career in Private Equity.
  • Opportunities for interaction with international deal team professionals and potential for travel for assignments.
  • A supportive environment that fosters career growth and development, with potential for conversion to higher roles based on performance.
Expected start date:
  • Q2/Q3 2026

Inclusion at EQT

Our vision for EQT employees is to build high performing & engaged teams. Our competitive edge comes from fostering an environment where every individual feels valued, empowered, and motivated to drive business impact. Our commitment to inclusion is not just about fairness; We understand and believe that being a great place to work drives the best performance.At EQT, inclusion is a business imperative and it's embedded into our talent strategy, decision-making, and culture to ensure that every individual and team operates at their full potential. By doing so, we unlock better collaboration, stronger innovation, and superior investment outcomes.About EQT

EQT is a purpose-driven global investment organization focused on active ownership strategies. With a Nordic heritage and a global mindset, EQT has a track record of over three decades of developing companies across multiple geographies, sectors and strategies. EQT has investment strategies covering all phases of a business’ development, from start-up to maturity. EQT has EUR ‌​​270 billion in total assets under management (EUR ‌​​‌141 billion in fee-generating assets under management), within two business segments – Private Capital and Real Assets.

With its roots in the Wallenberg family’s entrepreneurial mindset and philosophy of long-term ownership, EQT is guided by a set of strong values and a distinct corporate culture. EQT manages and advises funds and vehicles that invest across the world with the mission to future-proof companies, generate attractive returns and make a positive impact with everything EQT does. EQT has offices in more than 25 countries across Europe, Asia and the Americas and has more than 1,900 employees.

More info: www.eqtgroup.com
Follow EQT on , ,  and

jobs.title: 2026 Point72 Academy Insight Program – Japan
jobs.description:
2026年 Point72 Academy インサイト・プログラム – 東京

Point72 Academy Insight Program は、投資のキャリアに関心を持つ大学生を対象とした、対面形式・1週間の職場体験プログラムです。専攻分野を問わず、あらゆる学問分野の学生に向けて設計されています。

プログラム概要
本プログラムは、2026年12月〜2028年9月の間に卒業予定の学生を対象に、Point72のビジネスや企業文化について理解を深めていただくことを目的としています。また、より多くの学生に投資業界でのキャリアを知っていただき、そのキャリアパスへのアクセス機会を広げることを目指しています。

東京オフィスでの5日間を通じて、金融市場への理解を深めながら、Point72 Academyで働く社員の日々の業務を間近で見学していただきます。

Point72で働くとはどのようなことかを実際に体感しながら、当社のビジネスの基礎を学び、投資アイデアがどのように生み出されるのかを理解するとともに、チームメンバーと交流する機会が得られます。

また、Point72 Academyアソシエイトの役割についての理解を深めるとともに、Academyのリーダーから直接フィードバックを受ける機会も提供されます。

インサイト・プログラムでは、以下の内容に取り組んでいただきます。

  • ビジネスへの理解：Point72のプロフェッショナルとの交流を通じて、Point72における投資アナリストの役割や仕事内容について理解を深めます。
  • 観察と振り返り：Academyのトレーニング／指導セッションを見学し、その内容を踏まえて考察を行ったうえで、Point72 Academyのコーチと意見交換を行います。
  • 関心分野の探究：双方向型の学習セッションやスキルワークショップに参加することで、自身の強みや関心がどの分野に最も適しているのかを探ります。
  • ネットワークの構築：プログラム期間中、金融分野に関心を持つ他の学生とともに取り組むことで、ネットワークを広げていきます。

求める人物像

  • 専攻分野不問
    あらゆる学問分野の学生を歓迎します。専攻や分野は選考の決定要因ではありません。チームで円滑に協働できる力、高い誠実さ、知的好奇心、リーダーシップの素養を備え、金融市場に強い関心を持つ方を求めています。
  • 金融サービス業界への高い関心
    金融サービス業界に対して強い意欲・関心を有していることが望まれます。本プログラムの特性上、企業分析や株式分析に関心をお持ちの方を歓迎します。
  • 優れたコミュニケーション能力と主体性
    口頭および文書での高いコミュニケーション能力を持ち、自ら考え行動できる方。
  • 多様性に貢献できる資質・経験
    変化への適応力、異文化理解力、困難に向き合う強さ（レジリエンス）を備え、これまでの経験を通じて当社に多様な視点をもたらしていただける方。

応募資格・要件

以下の条件を満たす方を対象としています。

  • 日本国内の大学または大学院に在籍している方。
  • 日本語および英語を使用して、円滑にコミュニケーションが取れる方。
  • リサーチや問題解決に強い関心を持ち、高い知的好奇心をもって継続的に視野を広げられる方。
  • 高いプロフェッショナリズム、誠実さ、倫理観、そして卓越性を大切にし、学業および将来のキャリアに真摯に取り組める方。
  • 主体性を持ち、金融業界でのキャリアを探求することに高い意欲をお持ちの方。
  • 2026年8月24日（月）から28日（金）までの5日間、東京オフィスでの対面プログラムに参加可能であること。

※旅費支援について：東京都外の大学に在籍する学生に対しては、大学所在地から東京までの合理的な範囲の交通費を支給します。

応募スケジュール

  • 応募締切：2026年6月14日
  • 書類選考および所定の選考プロセスを経て、選考を通過された方には2026年7月27日までに結果をご連絡します。
  • 本プログラムは、2026年8月24日から28日の期間、東京にて対面形式で実施されます。

Point72について
Point72 は、スティーブン・A・コーエンが率いるグローバルなオルタナティブ投資会社です。

30年以上にわたる投資経験を活かし、私たちは投資プロフェッショナルの戦略的パートナーとして、優れたアイデアの実現を支援しています。現状に甘んじることなく、常に投資プロセスの革新を追求し、魅力的なリスク調整後リターンの創出を目指しています。また、投資家主導のカルチャーを育むとともに、人材一人ひとりの長期的な成長にコミットすることで、業界で最も優秀な人材を惹きつけ、育成・定着させることを重視しています。

Point72 Academyは、多様な視点や考え方をもたらす存在としてアナリストやポートフォリオ・マネージャーとしての成長・能力開発を支援しています。

The Point72 Academy’s Insight Program is an in-person, one-week work shadowing program designed to introduce undergraduate students from all academic backgrounds to a career in investing.

Point72 Academy Insight Program – Japan

This program is open to students planning to graduate between December 2026 to September 2028 who are eager to learn about our business and company culture. Our goal is to increase students’ awareness and access to a career in investing. Over the course of five days in our Tokyo office, you will gain exposure to financial markets and observe the day-to-day activities of our Academy professionals.  You’ll experience firsthand what it’s like to work at Point72 — learning the fundamentals of our business, seeing how investment ideas are developed, and interacting with our team. You’ll also gain insight into the role of a Point72 Academy Associate and receive feedback from Academy leadership.

During the Insight Program, you will:

  • Learn the Business: Gain an understanding of the role of a Point72 investment analyst by interacting with our professionals.
  • Observe and Reflect: Watch Academy teaching sessions, reflect on your observations and discuss your insights with a Point72 Academy Coach.
  • Explore your interests: Discover where your skills and passions fit best by immersing yourself in interactive learning sessions, and skills workshops.
  • Expand Your Network: Work with fellow students interested in finance throughout the program and broaden your network.

Who we’re looking for:

  • We welcome students from all academic disciplines. Your field of study or major is not a determining factor; we seek individuals who can effectively manage teamwork, exhibit integrity, possess intellectual curiosity, demonstrate leadership potential, and express a keen interest in the financial markets.
  • Students should have a high enthusiasm for the financial services industry. Interest in analyzing companies and looking at stocks is recommended given the nature of this program.
  • Candidates with excellent communication skills (verbal and written) and high levels of self-motivation.
  • Students who possess adaptability, cultural fluency, resilience, and past experiences that can contribute to the firm's diverse perspectives.

We want you to apply if you are:

  • Enrolled in university or graduate school in Japan.
  • Fluent in Japanese and English.
  • Fascinated by research and problem-solving, relentlessly curious, and constantly expanding your horizons.
  • Committed to continued success in your academic and professional career with the highest levels of professionalism, integrity, ethics, and excellence.
  • Highly self-driven and motivated to explore a career in finance.
  • Available to participate in-person in our Tokyo offices for five days from August 24 to 28, 2026.
  • Point72 will cover reasonable travel for students at universities located outside of Tokyo to and from your university location.

Application Timing:

  • Submit your application by June 14, 2026.
  • Following a brief assessment period, we will notify selected participants by July 27, 2026.
  • The program will run in-person in Tokyo from August 24 to 28, 2026.

The Point72 Academy Insight Program is conducted in both English and Japanese. 

Please note that any use of GenAI is strictly prohibited when completing this application.

ABOUT POINT72  

Point72 is a leading global alternative investment firm led by Steven A. Cohen. Building on more than 30 years of investing experience, Point72 seeks to deliver superior returns for its investors through fundamental and systematic investing strategies across asset classes and geographies. We aim to attract and retain the industry’s brightest talent by cultivating an investor-led culture and committing to our people’s long-term growth.   

We’re a firm that thrives on ideas, and we’ve found that the Point72 Academy has provided us with diverse viewpoints and perspectives, and we are able to offer support in their growth and development as analysts and portfolio managers.

companyDetails.description: Point72 Academy Insight Program は、投資のキャリアに関心を持つ大学生を対象とした、対面形式・1週間の職場体験プログラムです。専攻分野を問わず、あらゆる学問分野の学生に向けて設計されています。

jobs.title: 2026 Trader Summer Intern, Hong Kong (12-week program)
jobs.description:
A CAREER WITH POINT72’s Trading DEPARTMENT

Point72’s Asia Trading team is comprised of long/short equity and derivatives execution traders who support the firm’s multi-manager platform of investment professionals. If you are interested in pursuing a career in trading at a premier asset management firm, this role will provide you with unparalleled training and experience. We are looking for someone who is highly proficient, articulate, detail-oriented, and eager to be an important part of the financial markets.

ABOUT THE Asia Trading INTERNSHIP

If you are interested in pursuing a career in Trading, this 12-week summer internship will provide you with valuable experience at a complex global firm. During the internship, you will help support our Asia Trading team and gain exposure to:

  • Assisting with booking trades
  • Conducting industry and company specific research
  • Providing market updates, news, and fund flow summaries
  • Monitoring market shifts to inform the rest of the team
  • Identifying potential risk areas and working to prevent trade or operational errors
  • Monitoring price movement, volume, and trading flows
  • Point72’s ethical decision-making approach

SUMMER INTERNSHIP PROGRAM AT POINT72

In addition to the learning and development you’ll receive in your day-to-day role with your team, as a part of our summer internship program you will:  

  • Work alongside your fellow interns and be mentored by experienced professionals 
  • Meet your peers through designated intern programming, volunteer opportunities, and social events 
  • Attend sessions with senior leaders discussing their expertise and career paths 
  • Get hands-on access to the wealth of tools and resources that Point72 employees use everyday
  • Interact with groups and functions with unique responsibilities and perspectives on our industry
  • Develop technical, writing, and presentation skills
  • Participate in networking opportunities across the firm

WHAT'S REQUIRED

  • Expected degree from Dec 2026 to August 2027
  • Demonstrated academic success as reflected in a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.2
  • Knowledge of trading trends and products
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment 
  • Strong analytical, writing, verbal communication, and technical skills 
  • Strong attention to detail and demonstrated ability to work cooperatively with all levels of the team
  • Conduct yourself with professionalism and integrity at all times
  • Commitment to the highest ethical standards

ABOUT POINT72

Point72 is a leading global alternative investment firm led by Steven A. Cohen. Building on more than 30 years of investing experience, Point72 seeks to deliver superior returns for its investors through fundamental and systematic investing strategies across asset classes and geographies. We aim to attract and retain the industry’s brightest talent by cultivating an investor-led culture and committing to our people’s long-term growth. For more information, visit https://point72.com/.

Deadline for applications is 11:59PM HKT on December 31, 2025

jobs.title: 2027 Point72 Academy Investment Analyst Summer Internship Program - EMEA
jobs.description:
THE POINT72 ACADEMY SUMMER INTERNSHIP — EMEA

Are you a student interested in pursuing a career in investing? If you are graduating between December 2027 and July 2028, we invite you to apply for our 8-week summer internship, taking place from June to August 2027.

Launch your investing career in the Point72 Academy, an investment analyst training program that will provide you with the tools and knowledge to build a career in our ever-evolving industry. Since 2015, the Academy has graduated more than 230 analysts onto our platform, and some are portfolio managers today – as of March 2026.

The Point72 Academy Summer Internship is a combination of classroom instruction, portfolio casework, and mentorship. The program is designed to give you insights into finance, research, and the workings of the public markets and prepare you for a potential opportunity with the full-time Point72 Academy Associate Program.

During the internship, you will:

  • Gain exposure to the fundamental knowledge and skills necessary to contribute as an analyst, alongside your peers.
  • Receive training in accounting, modeling, presenting, and compliance.
  • Collaborate with an investment team, gaining firsthand experience of the daily life of an equities analyst and exposure to one of the sectors covered by our Portfolio Managers.
  • Work closely with Academy coaches, delving deeper into data analysis and preparing a final pitch on an investment idea.
  • Collaborate with Compliance, Information Security and the Office of the Co-CIO to discover efficient ways to harness AI in your investment processes and workflows

ABOUT POINT72

Point72 is a leading global alternative investment firm led by Steven A. Cohen. Building on more than 30 years of investing experience, Point72 seeks to deliver superior returns for its investors through fundamental and systematic investing strategies across asset classes and geographies. We aim to attract and retain the industry’s brightest talent by cultivating an investor-led culture and committing to our people’s long-term growth.

We’re a firm that thrives on ideas, and we’ve found that the Point72 Academy has provided us with diverse viewpoints and perspectives, and we are able to offer support in their growth and development as analysts and portfolio managers.

WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

We welcome students from all academic disciplines. Your field of study or major is not a determining factor; we seek individuals who can effectively manage teamwork, exhibit integrity, possess intellectual curiosity, demonstrate leadership potential, and express a keen interest in the financial markets.

Students should have a high enthusiasm for the financial services industry. Interest in analyzing companies and looking at stocks is recommended given the project-based nature of this program.

Candidates with excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, who are also self-motivated.

WE WANT YOU TO APPLY IF YOU ARE:

  • A current student who will graduate from university between December 2027 and July 2028.
  • Fascinated by research and problem-solving, relentlessly curious, and constantly expanding your horizons.
  • Committed to continued success in your academic and professional career with the highest levels of professionalism, integrity, and excellence.
  • Commitment to the highest ethical standards.
  • Highly self-driven and motivated to explore a career in finance.

WHAT TO EXPECT:

Your online application, essays, and resume will serve as an introduction to who you are as a candidate. Once you complete the online application, we will begin reviewing your profile.

Selected candidates will then receive an email invitation to participate in a case study, allowing you to showcase your experiences and demonstrate your business acumen.

Following submission and review of your case study, the next step would be an online assessment and virtual one on one interviews with various members of the Academy team, if selected.

There is no set application deadline for this position. Candidates are considered as they apply, until the program is at capacity. We strongly encourage you to apply and fulfill all requirements as soon as you feel ready to begin the hiring process. Please note, you may only submit one application to the Academy internship program globally, so only apply to the region you are most interested in.

Don't miss this opportunity to kickstart your career in the world of investing with the Point72 Academy.

Please note that any use of GenAI is strictly prohibited when completing this application.
