Title:  Associate Pricing & Valuation

Department: Pricing & Valuation

Location: London, UK

Barings is a leading global financial services firm dedicated to meeting the evolving investment and capital needs of our clients and customers. Through active asset management and direct origination, we provide innovative solutions and access to differentiated opportunities across public and private capital markets. A subsidiary of MassMutual, Barings maintains a strong global presence with business and investment professionals located across North America, Europe and Asia Pacific.

Job Summary

Barings is seeking a motivated individual with strong analytical skills and a strong attention to detail for the European Pricing/Valuation team. This individual will be responsible for key components of our diverse asset type valuations, including fixed income, equity, and private assets.

The role requires working directly across global departments within Barings, including traders, investment analysts, portfolio managers, fund accountants and other areas with operations. This position also includes review of Fair Value and Swing Pricing calculations prepared by team members.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Ensuring the Fair Valuation of Barings invested securities and the adherence to the Barings Pricing Policy
  • Preparing daily fair valuation instructions to the third party administrator
  • Pricing and monitoring of unlisted, illiquid, suspended, and distressed assets.
  • Calculating Swing Pricing factors and thresholds, and ensuring adherence to the Swing Pricing policies and procedures
  • Regular reporting to the Barings Pricing Committee, including attending and presenting at Committee meetings
  • Checking and verifying of team members’ inputs
  • Responding to global internal business partners as well as fund custodians and administrators.
  • Reviewing daily exception reports, validating output and overall review for accuracy. Team plays an integral role within the organization interacting with internal partners, portfolio managers, and trading desks.

Skills/Experience Required

  • Familiarity with a broad range of asset types
  • Fund Administration/Operations experience - Valuations related exposure a plus
  • Good understanding of global financial markets and current events
  • Intermediate knowledge of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel
  • Excellent communication skills and ability to work independently as well as on a team
  • Strong initiative, high attention to detail, and ability to respond quickly to issues are attributes a candidate must possess.

Desired Skills & Qualifications

  • Skills and experience using SQL, PowerBI, Aladdin, and VBA
  • IOC / IMC qualified or similar
  • Degree in Accounting, Finance or related field.
  • Swing Pricing and Fair Value experience

Job title: Portfolio Analytics, Senior Associate

Department: Portfolio Finance

Location: Charlotte, NC or Boston, MA

The Opportunity

The Portfolio Analytics Senior Associate is a key contributor towards operational effectiveness and efficiency leveraging technology-based solutions to streamline efficiencies, enable insightful program-level reporting, and support the reduction of investment and operational risk. This role sits within Barings Portfolio Finance’s Portfolio Analytics team, a team responsible for Data & Analytics, Operational Infrastructure, Management Reporting, Track Record, and Credit Modeling. The role operates at the intersection of finance and technology and serves as a key liaison with various internal partners.

The Team

The team is small, close-knit, and highly collaborative. Team members are motivated by a shared goal to originate attractive investment opportunities for third-party clients while addressing the unique financing needs of our customers. The team is primarily focused on originating investment-grade, senior secured loans to asset managers and the investment vehicles that they manage across a range of asset classes, including private equity, private credit, and real estate debt. The loans, directly originated by the Portfolio Finance team, are highly negotiated, and often involve complex structuring across various global jurisdictions. The team has originated over $38 billion in private investments since 2017 and recently transitioned to Barings from MassMutual.

Key Responsibilities

  • Perform routine data cleansing/pre-processing and automate data transformation processes.
  • Support with maintenance of BPF’s proprietary portfolio monitoring database and assessing data quality & integrity on a continuous basis.
  • Identify, analyze, and interpret financial data and uncover trends within large/complex data sets.
  • Identify various process improvement solutions and support with development of predictive analytic capabilities.
  • Critically evaluate information provided from multiple sources – both internal and external – and reconcile discrepancies to ensure sound data quality.
  • Produce (and automate where possible) regularly scheduled and ad-hoc management reports to enable decision making.
  • Participate in technology/automation initiatives to drive streamlined integration with borrowers, internal partners, and third-party vendors.
  • Support internal controls activities.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree (business and/or data science/analytics related) with 2 - 4 years of experience in data management within a financial context; solid financial acumen.
  • Minimum of 2 years’ experience in data analysis or reporting.

  • Experience with data visualization tools & best practices (hands on experience with PowerBi or Tableau preferred).

  • Strong knowledge of and experience with analytic tools and programming languages (Python/R, and SQL with VBA a plus).

  • Excellent command and proficiency of MS excel.

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, attention to detail, well-organized, self-starter, and able to multitask and deliver work products on short timeframes.
  • Willingness to learn and absorb complex lending structures and legal documents and translate economic and risk factors to financial models and tools used to manage the overall portfolio.
  • Experience with synthesizing and visualizing data for presentations.
  • Ability and openness to understanding operational, accounting, and legal aspects of investments.
  • Demonstrated interest in and experience with operational enablement through technology.
  • Excellent documentation and communication skills.
  • Control focused and attentive to detail.

Ideal Qualifications

  • Prior experience in investment reporting and analysis within private credit highly preferred.
  • Experience working with at least one alternative asset class and a good understanding of the risks and operations of others across the alternative investment landscape.

#LI-CM2

Base Salary Range: $90,000-105,000 and additional incentive compensation.

Requisite Skills

Data Analytics, Reporting and Analysis

Additional Skills

jobs.description:
At Barings, we are as invested in our associates as we are in our clients. Job Title: Commercial ABS, Credit Research Analyst

Corporate Title: Director

Department: Asset Backed Finance

Location: New York, Charlotte, or Boston

Overview

Barings is seeking a highly motivated and experienced professional to join its ABF (Asset-Backed Finance) team as a Director, Credit Research Analyst specializing in Commercial ABS transactions. The individual will be responsible for leading credit research, analysis, and underwriting of private and public market commercial ABS transactions with a dedicated focus on Transportation (Aviation, Rail), Digital Infrastructure (Data Centers, Fiber), and Esoteric ABS (Franchise, Whole Business) sectors.

This position requires deep expertise in evaluating complex asset-backed structures, conducting comprehensive credit analysis of underlying collateral, and making informed investment recommendations across the capital structure. The individual will work closely with portfolio managers, investment professionals, risk partners, and internal stakeholders to identify attractive investment opportunities, support transaction structuring and execution, and monitor portfolio performance. This role demands strong analytical capabilities, excellent communication skills, and the ability to develop credit views independently while collaborating effectively across a global team.

Key Responsibilities

Credit Research & Analysis

  • Lead comprehensive credit analysis and due diligence of commercial ABS transactions across Transportation, Digital Infrastructure, and Esoteric ABS  sectors.
  • Evaluate originator and servicer capabilities, business models, operational track records, and credit risk management frameworks.
  • Analyze collateral pools, performance data, vintage trends, and stratifications to assess credit quality and projected performance.
  • Assess legal and structural elements of ABS transactions, including waterfall mechanics, credit enhancement features, triggers, and covenants.
  • Develop independent credit views on asset fundamentals, including technology trends, regulatory developments, competitive dynamics, and business model risks.

Investment Evaluation & Execution

  • Screen, underwrite, and support approval of new investments in both public and private commercial ABS markets.
  • Prepare detailed investment memos, exhibits, and presentations for internal approval and investment committee processes.
  • Perform scenario, sensitivity, and stress analyses to evaluate structural resilience, downside protection, and expected returns across economic cycles.
  • Utilize cash flow models and analytical tools to form opinions on future asset and transaction performance.
  • Engage directly with issuers, arrangers, bankers, originators, and other transaction stakeholders throughout the investment process.

Portfolio Surveillance & Monitoring

  • Monitor ongoing performance of existing investments versus underwritten expectations and identify emerging risks or opportunities.
  • Analyze monthly/quarterly collateral and transaction performance reports, including delinquencies, defaults, losses, prepayments, and structural triggers.
  • Recommend actions based on asset performance trends, covenant compliance, and structural changes.
  • Maintain regular dialogue with issuers and servicers to stay informed on portfolio developments and operational updates.

Research, Thought Leadership & Collaboration

  • Author sector research, market commentary, and thematic reports on sector trends.
  • Contribute to analytical frameworks, underwriting standards, and investment guidelines for commercial ABS sectors.
  • Represent the firm at industry conferences, investor meetings, and client engagements.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional partners including portfolio management, risk, operations, legal, finance, and technology teams.
  • Mentor and support junior team members, fostering analytical rigor and professional development.

Qualifications

Education & Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, Mathematics, Accounting, or a related quantitative discipline preferred.
  • 7–15 years of relevant experience in structured finance, asset-backed securities, commercial credit analysis, or specialty finance, with demonstrated expertise in Transportation, Digital Infrastructure, and/or Esoteric ABS sectors.
  • Direct experience analyzing and underwriting commercial ABS transactions in both public and private markets is highly preferred.
  • Buy-side, sell-side, rating agency, or lending experience in asset-backed finance or structured credit is valued.

Technical Skills & Knowledge

  • Strong Excel-based financial modeling and analytical skills with a disciplined, well-structured, and detail-oriented approach.
  • Deep understanding of commercial ABS structures, collateral performance analytics, and credit fundamentals.
  • Experience with cash flow modeling platforms such as Intex, or similar tools.
  • Proficiency with Bloomberg, data analysis tools, and transaction databases.
  • Familiarity with Transportation, Digital Infrastructure, and/or Esoteric ABS asset classes, including knowledge of industry fundamentals, regulatory frameworks, and performance drivers.
  • Programming or data analytics skills (e.g., Python, SQL, R, VBA) are beneficial but not required.

Professional Attributes

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with demonstrated ability to clearly articulate complex credit views and analytical findings to senior stakeholders, investment committees, and external counterparties.
  • Strong attention to detail, sound judgment, and intellectual curiosity with the ability to independently develop credit opinions.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and projects simultaneously in a fast-paced, dynamic environment while maintaining high-quality analytical outputs.
  • Collaborative mindset with strong interpersonal skills and a team-oriented work ethic.
  • Results-oriented and self-motivated with a passion for credit analysis and structured finance.

Preferred Qualifications

  • CFA charter holder, CFA candidate, or other relevant professional designations (e.g., CAIA, FRM).
  • Advanced degree (MBA, MS Finance, or related field) is a plus.
  • Established relationships and credibility with market participants including issuers, arrangers, investors, and industry participants in Transportation, Digital Infrastructure, and Esoteric ABS markets.
  • Thoughtful perspectives on sector trends, technological developments, regulatory changes, and market dynamics impacting commercial ABS asset classes.

#LI-CM2

Base Salary Range: $170,000-215,000 plus additional incentives

Requisite Skills

Asset-Backed Securities (ABS), Credit Analysis, Credit Research, Investment Evaluation, Portfolio Monitoring

Additional Skills

Barings is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer; Minority/Female/Age/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity/Individual with Disability/Protected Veteran. We welcome all persons to apply.

Title: Director, Client Portfolio Management – Private Credit

Business Unit: Client Portfolio Management

Location: Singapore or Hong Kong

Barings is a leading global financial services firm dedicated to meeting the evolving investment and capital needs of our clients and customers. Through active asset management and direct origination, we provide innovative solutions and access to differentiated opportunities across public and private capital markets. A subsidiary of MassMutual, Barings maintains a strong global presence with business and investment professionals located across North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific.

Job Summary

We are looking for a Director, Client Portfolio Management to join in our Singapore office. Client Portfolio Managers work in close partnership and coordination with investment teams and sales as well as other stakeholders within the organization as subject matter experts, focusing on business development, client servicing and product development for certain investment strategies.

This position will preferably be located in Singapore, although candidates in Hong Kong will also be considered. The role will support all Barings private credit strategies (portfolio finance, infrastructure debt, asset-based finance, private placements, direct lending, capital solutions) with a primary focus on portfolio finance and infrastructure debt.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Work with product management, investment teams and sales teams to drive new and existing business development in private credit strategies (and portfolio finance and infrastructure debt in particular) in the APAC region.
  • Develop a close partnership with investment teams to deepen knowledge and expertise and be effective as a portfolio manager proxy in interactions with sales colleagues and clients. This will include:
    • Attending investment team meetings
    • Serving as an active participant in meetings with current and prospective clients
    • Responding in a timely manner to client and prospect requests
  • Communicate investment strategies internally and externally with the goal of growing assets under management
  • Help develop, maintain and prepare marketing and servicing materials for the team. This will include pitch books as well as scheduled and ad-hoc updates on private credit markets and specific aspects of an investment strategy (e.g. monthly commentaries, market reviews, market outlook, performance reviews, portfolio positioning etc).
  • Review and provide strategy specific responses to RFPs and DDQs
  • Identify, participate in and coordinate new product development and positioning initiatives
  • Create thought leadership materials in cooperation with investment teams and Barings Marketing staff

Qualifications

  • University degree
  • 7+ years of relevant work experience, ideally in an investment specialist/product strategist/client portfolio manager or similar role.
  • A passion for financial markets. Private credit markets experience a strong plus.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills including an ability to assess and convey market dynamics, portfolio strategy and risk factors
  • An aptitude for public speaking in a variety of settings and audience sizes
  • An entrepreneurial drive and desire to be a team player
  •  Strong organizational, facilitation and collaboration skills
  •  Willingness to travel regularly within the APAC region.

Requisite Skills

Additional Skills

Job Summary:

Responsible for supporting the loan activities of complex portfolio level loan facilities through the loan lifecycle.

Team Overview:

Barings Portfolio Finance provides customized, complex, highly negotiated, proprietary secured loans to private asset managers and manages the entire loan lifecycle.  As a member of Portfolio Finance’s loan asset management team you will support the investment team, borrowers, and external stakeholders – from origination to offboarding.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Assist with loan activities at closing and onboarding of new loan facilities including review of credit agreements, loan & servicing agreements, amendments, account control agreements, and closing/settlement statements in relation to Loan Asset Management functions and best practices
  • On-going servicing and other administration functions on serviced loans including compliance and covenant monitoring
  • Utilize financial models to record collateral data, test compliance metrics, and calculate waterfalls
  • Support treasury & investment operations activities through cash and reporting reconciliations
  • Ensure deliverables are managed to a high standard and within timelines

Qualifications

  • Degree in Business, Finance, related field or equivalent
  • 3+ years of relevant investment operations/support experience preferably with investing or servicing private equity, private real estate, or private credit and lending documents
  • Ability to develop credibility and build relationships with all internal and external stakeholders and partners
  • Excellent attention to detail  
  • Demonstrate exceptional project/time management, coordination, and organizational skills
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

Legal Documents, Relationship Building, Sharp Attention to Detail (Inactive), Team Working (Inactive), Time Management

Deal Closing, Microsoft Excel, Project Management

jobs.title: Finance Associate - GP
jobs.description:
BAIN CAPITAL OVERVIEW

With approximately $215 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,985+ employees in 24 offices on four continents. 

We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

GENERAL PARTNER GROUP

The General Partner Group is responsible for the operational, accounting, tax, and reporting aspects of each of the general partner entities.  These entities invest across all of the firm’s business units giving the team the unique opportunity to gain exposure throughout the company.  Members of the team also regularly collaborate with several internal departments, including fund accounting, tax, legal, compliance, HR, corporate, and IT, as well as third-party service providers such as the firm’s tax advisors. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

This position would primarily be responsible for assisting with the treasury, accounting, tax and fair market value reporting functions for General Partner entities associated with the Private Equity, Venture, Crypto, Double Impact, Life Sciences, Tech Opportunities and Insurance business Units. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to, the following:

  • Operations and administrative activity of the partnerships, including cash management, capital calls, cash distributions, cash reconciliations, bank account openings and related regulatory requirements

  • Carry, vesting, and profit/loss allocations to the partners and members of the General Partner entities

  • Quarterly and annual fair market value reporting, including supporting documentation for capital accounts, trial balances and other financial reconciliations

  • Quarterly tax estimates and annual tax books and reporting for the partnerships

  • Cash, book and tax journal entries into Investran, the third party portfolio management and accounting software

In addition, this position will also:

  • Assist in generating responses to investor inquiries and requests for information

  • Create reports for GP investors including fair market value, tax basis activity, and partnership investment reports

  • Interact with other teams within Finance to determine appropriate treatment of transactions and investment earnings

  • Work with all levels from Associates to Partners on projects to enhance the performance of the team, department, and firm

  • Opportunities for additional responsibilities where performance allows

GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

  • 1-2 years accounting experience in the financial service industry, with a solid understanding of investment partnerships preferred

  • Reliable and collaborative team member with strong sense of ownership and follow-through skills

  • Excellent project management and organizational skills; ability to effectively prioritize multiple projects with high attention to detail

  • Creative problem solving ability and sound judgment; ability to use own initiative and take responsibility for decisions and propose solutions

  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills with capacity to interact with all levels

  • Enjoys working in a service environment and understands the demands of a high paced, high quality firm

  • Ability to handle highly confidential and sensitive information with discretion

  • Works well independently and capitalizes on opportunities to learn and grow

  • Advanced MS Office, particularly Excel

DESIRED BACKGROUND/EDUCATION

·Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance

·Public accounting, partnership and/or financial services experience a plus, but not required

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $80,000 – 85,000

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Central Reporting
jobs.description:
BAIN CAPITAL CREDIT & SPECIAL SITUATIONS OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Credit founded as Sankaty Advisors in 1998, is a leading global credit specialist with approximately $41 billion in assets under management. Bain Capital Credit invests up and down the capital structure and across the spectrum of credit strategies, including leveraged loans, high-yield bonds, distressed debt, private lending, structured products, non-performing loans and equities. Bain Capital Special Situations has invested $35 billion in more than 900 deals across a balanced geographic mix of investments in North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific since its founding in 2002. We provide bespoke capital solutions to meet the diverse needs of companies, entrepreneurs, and asset owners - in all market cycles. The strategy brings together equity and credit expertise, as well as corporate and real asset expertise, to provide solutions which cannot be met by traditional providers.

BAIN CAPITAL OVERVIEW

With approximately $215 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,985+ employees in 24 offices on four continents.

We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

The senior associate will be an integral part of the team responsible for creating process efficiencies and ensuring data integrity across the Capital Markets business at Bain Capital. They will:

  • Develop and maintain automated solutions to address client, internal, and regulatory reporting needs, largely utilizing Alteryx, Python, and Power-BI.
  • Design, build, and maintain automated data workflows using Alteryx to streamline recurring reporting and reduce manual effort.
  • Provide internal consulting support across the business by diagnosing problems, structuring analyses, and recommending solutions that drive measurable impact.
  • Interact with Investor Relations, Finance, Operations, and Portfolio Managers to understand reporting/data aggregation needs when developing customized solutions.
  • Develop an expert understanding of the internal systems utilized for data entry and storage, as well as the flow of information through internal systems.
  • Engage in manual cleansing of historical data and formulation of new guidelines to enhance data accuracy for future entries.
  • Create visualizations for complex data models in PowerBI with the goal of simplifying understanding and reducing manual efforts for end users.
  • Address ad-hoc problem-solving needs, develop creative solutions, and own end user testing and implementation.

GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

  • 2 - 4+ years of full-time experience in data analytics within a finance or accounting setting, preferably at an investment firm.
  • Proficient in PowerBI development, including semantic model relationships, database queries (preferably Snowflake), writing measures in DAX, and advanced visualization techniques.
  • A foundational understanding of credit funds and portfolio management.
  • Demonstrated project management skills and excellent organizational skills; ability to effectively prioritize multiple projects with diligent attention to detail.
  • Self-starter with initiative. Ability to work unsupervised.
  • Strong interpersonal skills with capacity to interact with outside parties and personnel across all levels of the firm.
  • Collaborate daily with team members to brainstorm approaches, challenge assumptions, and improve deliverables through shared iteration.
  • Strong ability to think outside the box to develop solutions to Frequent / Challenging / Unique ad-hoc requests to deliver the desired information, often starting from a blank slate.

DESIRED BACKGROUND/EDUCATION

  • A bachelor’s degree in data analytics, accounting, finance, or a related field is preferred.
  • Strong analytical background utilizing PowerBI to summarize large datasets.
  • Proficient writing code in Python, DAX, and SQL
  • A keen interest in technology and its application in finance, with the capacity to propose and implement process improvements.
  • CPA or equivalent qualification a plus.
  • Work toward or an interest in pursuing a CFA charter a plus.

CAREER DEVELOPMENT OPPORTUNITIES

  • The role offers ample opportunity for growth into senior finance or analytical roles within the firm.
  • Utilize PowerBI to create and refine interactive dashboards, presenting asset and portfolio data effectively.
  • Ongoing exposure to advanced data analysis tools and methodologies will be provided.
  • Potential to contribute to firm-wide strategic initiatives and influence data governance practices.

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $100,000 - $120,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: Aviation Asset Management Associate
jobs.description:
With approximately $205 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit & Capital Markets, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,950+ employees in 24 offices on four continents.

BAIN CAPITAL SPECIAL SITUATIONS OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Special Situations has $24 billion in assets under management and has invested $19 billion in ~250 deals across a balanced geographic mix of investments in North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific since its founding in 2018. We provide bespoke capital solutions to meet the diverse needs of companies, entrepreneurs, and asset owners - in all market cycles. The strategy brings together equity and credit expertise, as well as corporate and real asset expertise, to provide solutions which cannot be met by traditional providers or others funds within Bain Capital. Our dedicated, global team of approximately 145 investment and portfolio professionals contribute the local expertise and capabilities that enable these diverse investments.

Aviation is one of our key focus areas, both today and through the market cycle. Over our history, we have invested close to $5 billion in Special Situations aviation investments. Acquiring physical aircraft, including commercial, cargo, and private jets, has been a particular focus with over $2 billion invested in this sub-sector over the past few years. Going forward, we expect to build off our industry-leading track record to continue to grow our presence in aircraft hard assets, including investing dedicated capital for this investment strategy.  

PORTFOLIO GROUP OVERVIEW

Bain Capital has a dedicated global Portfolio Group within the firm’s Special Situations business. The Portfolio Group is responsible for fund and asset management and leads the strategy and analysis on fund construction, risk, and opportunities for the Global Special Situations business.

The Portfolio Group works side-by-side with Special Situations leadership and deal teams and provides extensive support, whenever and wherever needed on analytical, strategic, and operating issues. These capabilities have helped many Bain Capital companies develop and execute strategies to move swiftly to gain competitive advantage and achieve superior growth. We currently own ~$7 billion of aircraft assets across several platforms. Given this scale and complexity, the Portfolio Group is a highly valued and fully integrated piece of our aircraft strategy. We are currently hiring an Associate to work as part of the Portfolio Group focused on aircraft hard assets in our New York City office.

RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Provide analytical support to inform investment decisions, including new asset acquisitions, financings, sales, etc.
  • Act as a thought partner to the deal team, leveraging modeling, data, and analytics
  • Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders around all key asset management actions
  • Work on all aspects of the portfolio, including new deals, ongoing portfolio work, and ultimately value realization
  • Focus on modeling, data analytics, industry research, and internal/external reporting, all with the goal of optimizing investment outcomes
  • Develop analytical tools and maintain systems to assist in strategy setting, returns optimization, and capital formation
  • Attend board meetings, global industry events, etc.

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Strong academic credentials (BS/BS in finance, economics, accounting and/or related degree preferred)
  • 2-4 years of data analytics, risk analytics, portfolio management, or strategic finance experience; prior background in aviation or aircraft leasing is a strong plus but not required
  • High proficiency in Excel; ability to build and iterate analytical tools and dashboard with experience through former roles, internships or coursework in managing datasets and running financial models / scenario analytics
  • Ability to synthesize large, ambiguous data sets into clear insights
  • High proficiency in Powerpoint with ability to effectively communicate insights from analyses to diverse stakeholders
  • Strong interpersonal skills with ability to interact with outside parties and personnel across all levels of the firm
  • Strong oral and written communication skills with ability to distill analysis into crisp recommendations

Compensation

Expected Base Salary Range: $125,000 – $150,000

This is the expected base salary range for this position. Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

TECH OPPORTUNITIES OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Tech Opportunities (https://www.baincapitaltechopportunities.com/) is an affiliate of Bain Capital established to invest in growing technology businesses. We partner with exceptional entrepreneurs and management teams to help them succeed through critical phases of growth and expansion. We provide flexible capital, strategic and operational resources, and deep industry expertise backed by the global reach of the Bain Capital platform. Tech Opps focuses on large and growing end markets: application software, infrastructure and cybersecurity, healthcare IT, and fintech and payments. The fund targets companies with innovative or disruptive technology and supports their transformational growth.

Investments are deployed globally and across a broad array of archetypes, including mid-market control buyouts, late-stage growth capital, tactical opportunities, and cross-platform investments alongside other parts of Bain Capital. 

OPPORTUNITY

We’re seeking a Senior Finance Associate to support fund accounting oversight, investor reporting, and operational finance for Bain Capital Tech Opportunities. In this role, you will assist in reviewing financial statements and reporting produced by our third-party fund administrator, support audit coordination, and collaborate with deal teams and Investor Relations to help deliver accurate, timely financial reporting and maintain strong controls. Success in the first 6–12 months includes contributing to consistent, timely financial reporting with clear reconciliations, supporting a smooth year-end audit, and assisting with reporting automation or process improvement initiatives while building familiarity with our systems and processes.

POSITION OVERVIEW

Great opportunity with room to grow career wise in a fast paced/high achieving fund at Bain Capital. Will have exposure to and collaborate on all aspects of finance/operational functions of Bain Tech Opportunities funds (currently 3 funds). Assist with the oversight, preparation, review, and execution of the following activities for the Tech Opportunities Business Unit:

  • Review of quarterly NAVs and capital reporting to investors.

  • Review quarterly financial statements, management fee and carry calculations, as well as other deliverables for investor reporting.

  • Support the year-end audit process with external accountants and help facilitate related requests.

  • Support the third-party fund administrator across reporting and operational activities.

  • Assist in monitoring cash availability and fund flows.

  • Work with deal staff and CFO to assist in the preparation of quarterly portfolio company valuations & prepare summarized data for the Valuation Committee.

  • Respond to requests and inquiries of limited partners and their representatives.

  • Review and analyze portfolio company financials in preparation for quarterly portfolio review meetings

  • Work with Investor Relations in preparation of presentation materials, information posted to investor website, DD requests and fundraising documents.

  • Analysis of various fund activities to ensure compliance with partnership agreements.

  • Work with the Finance team and CFO on projects to enhance the product of the team, department and firm.

  • Other ad hoc responsibilities.

GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

  • 3+ years’ experience at an Investment Advisor and/or public accounting role preferred. Private and public exposure a plus.

  • Excellent project management and organizational skills; ability to effectively prioritize multiple projects with the highest attention to detail.

  • Creative problem-solving ability and sound judgment.

  • Strong interpersonal skills with capacity to interact with all levels.

  • Team player.

  • Comfort working across a variety of technology platforms, including CRMs, HR and finance systems (e.g., Workday), AI-powered tools, and other enterprise software; demonstrated ability to quickly learn and adapt to new technologies.

  • Experience with or curiosity about AI-powered tools for financial analysis, reporting automation, and workflow optimization; demonstrated ability to leverage technology to drive process efficiencies.

  • Strong data literacy, including the ability to work with large datasets and familiarity with data visualization or business intelligence tools (e.g., Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar).

EDUCATION

  • Four-year college degree with a strong academic background.

  • Major in accounting, finance or related degree required preferred.

  • CPA, CFA or equivalent qualification a plus.

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $95,000 – 110,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Established in 1984, Bain Capital is one of the world's leading private investment firms with approximately $185 billion in assets under management. Bain Capital manages a range of strategies including private equity, credit, public equity, venture capital, real estate, life sciences, and impact investments. Headquartered in Boston, Bain Capital has offices in Chicago, Dublin, Guangzhou, Hong Kong, Lisbon, London, Luxembourg, Madrid, Mauritius, Melbourne, Mumbai, Munich, New York, Osaka, Palo Alto, San Francisco, Seoul, Shanghai, Singapore, Sydney, and Tokyo.

Bain Capital Credit Overview
Bain Capital Credit is a leading global credit specialist with approximately $53 billion in assets under management. We invest across the full spectrum of credit strategies, including asset-based finance, direct lending, leveraged loans, high-yield bonds, non-performing loans, real estate and other hard assets and equities. Through a wide diversity of mandates, we have the ability to invest in every level of a company or asset’s capital structure from secured debt to equity and can provide holistic solutions to growing companies with unique financing needs.

Bain Capital Credit has a world-class team of over 100 investment professionals with extensive experience analyzing and managing credit investments.

We are seeking a Sector Head at the Vice President or Director level to join our Credit team in Boston or New York office.

Responsibilities Include:

  • Lead a team that analyzes, evaluates, and monitors investments across our strategies

  • Conceive and execute in-depth industry, competitive, and financial analyses in order to formulate and test differential investment hypotheses 

  • Perform company and industry research: visit company management, do field research (with customers, suppliers, and others likely to have unique insight into a company’s or industry’s fundamentals), and attend appropriate industry/Wall Street conferences

  • Make proactive buy/sell recommendations and communicate investment views effectively with appropriate team members

  • Identify and evaluate attractive investment ideas, primarily in the public markets, but also selective private market investments

  • Manage the workload, responsibilities and development of analysts/associates

Qualifications:

  • Strong academic credentials and a demonstrated track record of successful work experience

  • 6+ plus years of professional experience ideally in an investment management firm, investment banking, sell-side research, public or private investing, and/or management consulting role

  • A fundamentals-driven, “bottom up” investing style with strong analytical/modeling capability

  • Experience interacting with and assessing senior management teams; demonstrated relationship-building and networking skills 

  • Ability to anticipate and identify company, industry, and market developments and support insights with research and analysis

  • Excellent communication skills; able to synthesize complicated analyses concisely and to articulate insightful conclusions compellingly.  Open to spirited debate over ideas

  • A passion for investing

  • Proven ability to work collaboratively in team environments

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $200,000 – $275,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: Senior Finance Associate - Coinvest
jobs.description:
BAIN CAPITAL OVERVIEW:

With approximately $215 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,985+ employees in 24 offices on four continents. We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights. Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

COINVEST FINANCE GROUP

Significant alignment with LPs has been one of the cornerstones of Bain Capital’s strategy since the firm was founded. This alignment is achieved through the firm’s extensive coinvest program, through which employees, collectively, have been, and continue to be among the largest investors in nearly every Bain Capital investment across all business units. The Bain Capital Coinvest Finance Group is a team of 20+ individuals responsible for the operational, accounting, tax, and reporting aspects of the coinvest portfolio. With significant investments across the entire Bain Capital platform, the portfolio provides the team with the unique opportunity to gain exposure to all of the firm’s business units. Members of the team regularly collaborate with members of nearly every internal department in the company, including investment professionals from all business units, all internal finance departments, tax, legal, HR, compliance, and IT, as well as the firm’s outside advisors, including attorneys, banks, custodians, and accounting and tax professionals. This role offers broad exposure across Bain Capital’s global investment platform, meaningful ownership of complex partnership structures and related finance processes, regular interactions with professionals across the Firm, and a dynamic day-to-day within a highly collaborative team environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES FOR THIS POSITION

Responsible for a number of the co-invest partnerships and working with and supervising staff on the following:

  • Ownership of quarterly and year-end accounting and reporting for assigned partnerships

  • Oversight and review of daily transactional activity for large portfolios of funds, including capital calls and distributions, with responsibility for identifying issues, ensuring appropriate controls, and escalating issues as needed

  • Preparation and review of annual tax basis books, including tracking and review of K-1 activity and coordination with internal and external tax teams

  • Management of fund administrative activities including cash management and treasury responsibilities, capital and custody management, cash and stock distributions, monthly cash reconciliations, and tax withholding analysis

  • Active collaboration with stakeholders ranging from Associates to Partners on cross-functional initiatives to enhance the reporting, controls, and the overall coinvest platform

  • Participation in process improvement, documentation, and systems-related initiatives, leveraging prior experience to enhance efficiency and scalability

GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

  • 3+ years of accounting experience with investment companies with tax exposure, and/or public accounting with audit and/or tax experience

  • Solid understanding of investment partnerships and partnership accounting concepts

  • Strong project management and organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines while maintaining a high level of accuracy and attention to detail

  • Demonstrated ability to exercise sound judgment, operate with increasing autonomy, and proactively identify, assess, and resolve issues

  • Strong interpersonal skills with capacity to interact with all levels

  • Understands and enjoys the demands of a service-oriented, deadline-driven environment

  • Capable of handling highly confidential and sensitive information

  • Advanced MS Office, particularly Excel

DESIRED BACKGROUND/EDUCATION

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance

  • CPA preferred, Master’s Degree a plus

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $95,000 – 110,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

PRIVATE EQUITY OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Partners is the private equity affiliate of Bain Capital. Over the past 31 years, The Private Equity Group has made over 280 investments and has 15 active global buyout funds. Our private equity activity primarily includes leveraged buyouts and growth capital invested across a wide variety of sectors. Bain Capital has found that a combination of a strong management team, sound fundamental business analysis, and a focused strategy can substantially improve a company’s income as well as its long-term strategic value.

Bain Capital Partners seeks opportunities to invest in market leading companies poised for dynamic change and to back outstanding management teams to implement that change. We believe the most critical ingredients in a company’s success are the management team and the alignment of management incentives with those of the investors. Our portfolio companies have significant management-equity ownership as a key part of the investment strategy. Bain Capital professionals are significant investors in each transaction, which achieves an alignment of goals with portfolio company management teams.

The group has developed significant expertise in several business areas including technology, healthcare, retail, telecommunications, and industrial and consumer products.  Transaction types include leveraged acquisitions, expansion capital, growth buyouts, turnarounds, consolidation plays, private buyouts, public company recapitalizations, convertible preferred investments, and financial restructurings.

·         Working with Finance Managers, Directors and VP’s on projects to enhance the product of the team, department and firm.

·         2-4 years accounting experience

·         CPA preferred

·         Excellent project management and organizational skills; ability to effectively prioritize multiple projects with the highest attention to detail

·         Creative problem solving ability and sound judgment – ability to use own initiative and take responsibility for decisions

jobs.title: Associate, Real Estate Portfolio Management
jobs.description:
Role: Portfolio Management Associate, Bain Capital Real Estate

Location: Boston, MA

Bain Capital Overview

With approximately $205 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit & Capital Markets, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,950+ employees in 24 offices on four continents.


We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.
 

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes.

Real Estate Overview

Since 2010, senior members of the Bain Capital Real Estate (“BCRE”) team have refined a thematic and nimble business model, focused on thesis-driven and resilient portfolio construction, designed to capture long-term demand growth and changing customer needs and preferences for real estate. Our team, which is led by Ryan Cotton, Partner, and comprises over 100 dedicated professionals, has been thoughtfully constructed to capture market opportunities at scale. To date, our strategy has been utilized for the acquisition of over 600 assets with total equity commitments of $9.8 billion. This includes, but is not limited to, acquisitions across BCRE Fund I ($1.5 billion, 2018 vintage), BCRE Fund II ($3.0 billion, 2020 vintage), the BCRE Life Science Fund (~$800 million, 2021 vintage), and BCRE Fund III (Raising, 2023 vintage) value add-plus real estate funds focused predominantly on thematic investments in North America. We believe that while our business model is built to succeed in a broad range of environments, it is particularly well suited for the current period of volatility, with distress likely to create attractive deal flow over the near to medium term.

Averaging over 20 years of applicable experience, Bain Capital Real Estate’s 13 Partners and Managing Directors have long and varied careers creating and managing innovative real estate and private equity investment platforms across an array of investment environments. Our senior team approaches decision-making with their expertise in acquisitions, asset management, portfolio management, financing, investment sales, investment banking, financial reporting, legal, engineering, and development management, in mind. They also have direct lines into long-time industry leaders as well as up-and-coming real estate players across a variety of asset classes and specialties. The team draws upon this foundation of complementary expertise and experience to source opportunities, drive value, and mitigate risk. As part of Bain Capital, the real estate team leverages the collective scale, experience, and network of the firm’s global platform to augment sourcing efforts as well as to deepen insights and understanding of underlying secular themes driving demand and changing customer needs and desires across sectors germane to its current portfolio as well as future investment themes.

BCRE Portfolio Management

The Portfolio Management (PM) team is responsible for driving value, managing risk, and shaping fund performance across Bain Capital Real Estate’s portfolios. This role works closely with PM leadership to implement tools, processes, and insights that enhance decision-making and fund returns. The hire will play an essential role in liquidity planning, performance monitoring, roll-forward underwriting cycles, business-plan tracking, and cross-functional coordination with Investment, Asset Management, and Finance teams.

Key Responsibilities

Value Creation & Risk Management

  • Support PM leads in identifying risks, opportunities, and drivers of fund performances
  • Help synthesize insights on return drivers, concentration, underperformance risks, and high-impact opportunities

Tools, Reporting & Analytics

  • Help build and maintain tools that track liquidity, equity needs, reserves, refinancing risk, and deal-level convexity
  • Apply data analytics and programming experience (Python, SQL preferred) to enhance tooling, automate processes, and extract insights from large, complex datasets
  • Assist in developing monitoring tools for performance vs. underwriting and roll-forward projections
  • Maintain and enhance the PM reporting package to deliver clear, actionable insights
  • Contribute to ongoing return-enhancement projects (e.g., recycling, subline utilization, gross-to-net improvement)

Process & Portfolio Management

  • Support implementation of consistent processes across Investment, Asset Management, and Finance (e.g. hold/sell, budgets, valuations, roll-forwards, capital calls)
  • Help drive standardization across strategies—particularly around sale processes and hold/sell frameworks
  • Maintain watchlists and assist with follow-ups for high-impact or high-risk assets/JVs
  • Track business plan milestones, JV updates, debt/refi activity, and sale readiness

Roll-Forward (Re-underwriting) Leadership

  • Play a key operational and analytical role in the semiannual roll-forward process
  • Help coordinate cross-team inputs, manage timelines, and ensure consistency of assumptions and outputs
  • Contribute to iterative improvements that enhance efficiency, comparability and insights each cycle

Fund Composition, Reporting & Strategic Insights

  • Support reporting on fund composition, exposures, asset concentration, and relative risk/return
  • Assist in preparing portfolio reviews and Partner updates with clear perspectives on performance, risks, and liquidity
  • Help refine the PM frameworks, tools, and processes as the portfolio and team evolve

Qualifications

Experience & Background

  • 2–4 years in consulting, investment banking, private equity, or real estate investing
  • Real estate experience preferred but not required
  • Demonstrated ability to work independently and drive processes without a roadmap

Analytical & Technical Skills

  • Strong financial and quantitative skills
  • High proficiency in Excel; ability to build and iterate analytical tools and dashboards
  • Programming/data analytics experience preferred (Python, R, Java, SQL, Claude Code, other LLMs)
  • Comfort synthesizing large, ambiguous data sets into clear insights

Attributes & Working Style

  • Tenacious, proactive, high ownership mentality
  • Structured thinker who can bring clarity to ambiguity
  • Strong communicator able to distill analysis into crisp recommendations
  • Effective collaborator across Investment, Asset Management, Finance, and senior leadership

Compensation

Expected Base Salary Range: $125,000 – $150,000

This is the expected base salary range for this position. Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: PM Associate, Private Credit
jobs.description:
PM Associate, Private Credit

Established in 1984, Bain Capital is one of the world's leading private investment firms with approximately $105 billion in assets under management. Bain Capital manages a range of strategies including private equity, public equity, credit, venture capital, real estate, life sciences, and impact investments.  Our competitive advantage is grounded in a diligence-intensive, value-added investment approach that enables the firm to deliver industry-leading returns for its investors. Headquartered in Boston, Bain Capital has offices in Chicago, Dublin, Guangzhou, Hong Kong, London, Madrid, Melbourne, Mumbai, Munich, New York, Palo Alto, San Francisco, Seoul, Shanghai, Sydney, and Tokyo.

BAIN CAPITAL CREDIT OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Credit is a leading global credit specialist with approximately $53 billion in assets under management.  We invest across the full spectrum of credit strategies, including asset-based finance, direct lending, leveraged loans, high-yield bonds, non-performing loans, real estate and other hard assets and equities.  Through a wide diversity of mandates, we have the ability to invest in every level of a company or asset’s capital structure from secured debt to equity and can provide holistic solutions to growing companies with unique financing needs.

Our investment process is characterized by detailed business and financial analysis and a culture of vigorous debate.  We combine a consulting-based approach to industry, competitor, and business analysis with an in-depth examination of a target company’s financial performance and capital structure.  Our goal is to select the best industries, the best companies, and the best relative value among the many securities each company offers.

Bain Capital Credit has a world-class team of over 110 investment professionals with extensive experience analyzing and managing high-yield investments. 

PRIVATE CREDIT GROUP

Bain Capital Credit’s Private Credit Group evaluates investment opportunities across the spectrum in the middle market, or typically companies with $10-150M of EBITDA located in North America, Europe and Asia Pacific.  With a dedicated global team of over 40 investment professionals, we can lead investments across the capital structure in senior secured first lien, second lien and unitranche loans, subordinated debt, and preferred or common equity co-investments. Our dedicated global team affords us the ability to diligence the most complex situations and provides private capital to those companies. 

PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT TEAM

The Portfolio Management Team works directly with senior management on group strategy and fund positioning, with a focus on asset allocation, return optimization, risk management, liability management and fund analysis. The Private Credit Group has $21 billion AUM today across two business development companies, Bain Capital Specialty Finance, Inc. (NYSE: BCSF) and Bain Capital Private Credit (BCPC), the flagship Global Direct Lending and Middle Market Credit Partners funds, as well as several other separately managed accounts, Private Credit CLOs and structured fund products. We are looking to hire an Associate.

RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE

  • Analyze current portfolio positioning and capitalization across funds and managed accounts in the firm’s Private Credit strategies

  • Support in evaluating, structuring, and executing on funds, structured funds (rated notes, collateralized fund obligations), financing structures (SPVs, CLOs) and other structured products

  • Contribute to the assessment and execution of strategic growth planning initiatives within the Private Credit Group

  • Build and maintain complex financial models of the Private Credit Group’s funds and managed accounts

  • Generate the analytical framework used by the portfolio management team, investors, and other related parties

  • Conduct a wide range of research and analysis for use in portfolio management, investor requests and internal analytics

  • Communicate with senior management and investors on current portfolio construction and return information

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Strong academic credentials and a demonstrated track record of professional high performance

  • Minimum 3-5 years of experience with a strong preference for prior credit market experience including leveraged lending, private credit or structured credit experience

  • Excellent communication skills; able to synthesize complicated analyses concisely and articulate conclusions in a compelling manner.  Open to spirited debate over ideas.

  • Strong financial statement analysis and modeling abilities. Strong understanding of public and private capital markets, corporate securities and capital structures

  • High agility and an ability to multi-task and work across the organization on a wide range of workstreams and deliverables.

  • Experience with middle market direct lending, or CLO or other structured / financing products is a plus

Compensation:
Expected Annual Base Salary $140,000-150,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,985+ employees in 24 offices on four continents. 

We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

GENERAL PARTNER GROUP

The General Partner Group is responsible for the operational, accounting, tax, and reporting aspects of each of the general partner entities.  These entities invest across all of the firm’s business units giving the team the unique opportunity to gain exposure throughout the company.  Members of the team also regularly collaborate with several internal departments, including fund accounting, tax, legal, compliance, HR, corporate, and IT, as well as third-party service providers such as the firm’s tax advisors. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

This position would primarily be responsible for assisting with the treasury, accounting, tax and fair market value reporting functions for General Partner entities associated with the Private Equity, Venture, Crypto, Double Impact, Life Sciences, Tech Opportunities and Insurance business Units. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to, the following:

  • Operations and administrative activity of the partnerships, including cash management, capital calls, cash distributions, cash reconciliations, bank account openings and related regulatory requirements

  • Carry, vesting, and profit/loss allocations to the partners and members of the General Partner entities

  • Quarterly and annual fair market value reporting, including supporting documentation for capital accounts, trial balances and other financial reconciliations

  • Quarterly tax estimates and annual tax books and reporting for the partnerships

  • Cash, book and tax journal entries into Investran, the third party portfolio management and accounting software

In addition, this position will also:

  • Assist in generating responses to investor inquiries and requests for information

  • Create reports for GP investors including fair market value, tax basis activity, and partnership investment reports

  • Interact with other teams within Finance to determine appropriate treatment of transactions and investment earnings

  • Work with all levels from Associates to Partners on projects to enhance the performance of the team, department, and firm

  • Opportunities for additional responsibilities where performance allows

GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

  • 1-2 years accounting experience in the financial service industry, with a solid understanding of investment partnerships preferred

  • Reliable and collaborative team member with strong sense of ownership and follow-through skills

  • Excellent project management and organizational skills; ability to effectively prioritize multiple projects with high attention to detail

  • Creative problem solving ability and sound judgment; ability to use own initiative and take responsibility for decisions and propose solutions

  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills with capacity to interact with all levels

  • Enjoys working in a service environment and understands the demands of a high paced, high quality firm

  • Ability to handle highly confidential and sensitive information with discretion

  • Works well independently and capitalizes on opportunities to learn and grow

  • Advanced MS Office, particularly Excel

DESIRED BACKGROUND/EDUCATION

·Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance

·Public accounting, partnership and/or financial services experience a plus, but not required

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $80,000 – 85,000

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Central Reporting
jobs.description:
BAIN CAPITAL CREDIT & SPECIAL SITUATIONS OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Credit founded as Sankaty Advisors in 1998, is a leading global credit specialist with approximately $41 billion in assets under management. Bain Capital Credit invests up and down the capital structure and across the spectrum of credit strategies, including leveraged loans, high-yield bonds, distressed debt, private lending, structured products, non-performing loans and equities. Bain Capital Special Situations has invested $35 billion in more than 900 deals across a balanced geographic mix of investments in North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific since its founding in 2002. We provide bespoke capital solutions to meet the diverse needs of companies, entrepreneurs, and asset owners - in all market cycles. The strategy brings together equity and credit expertise, as well as corporate and real asset expertise, to provide solutions which cannot be met by traditional providers.

BAIN CAPITAL OVERVIEW

With approximately $215 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,985+ employees in 24 offices on four continents.

We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

The senior associate will be an integral part of the team responsible for creating process efficiencies and ensuring data integrity across the Capital Markets business at Bain Capital. They will:

  • Develop and maintain automated solutions to address client, internal, and regulatory reporting needs, largely utilizing Alteryx, Python, and Power-BI.
  • Design, build, and maintain automated data workflows using Alteryx to streamline recurring reporting and reduce manual effort.
  • Provide internal consulting support across the business by diagnosing problems, structuring analyses, and recommending solutions that drive measurable impact.
  • Interact with Investor Relations, Finance, Operations, and Portfolio Managers to understand reporting/data aggregation needs when developing customized solutions.
  • Develop an expert understanding of the internal systems utilized for data entry and storage, as well as the flow of information through internal systems.
  • Engage in manual cleansing of historical data and formulation of new guidelines to enhance data accuracy for future entries.
  • Create visualizations for complex data models in PowerBI with the goal of simplifying understanding and reducing manual efforts for end users.
  • Address ad-hoc problem-solving needs, develop creative solutions, and own end user testing and implementation.

GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

  • 2 - 4+ years of full-time experience in data analytics within a finance or accounting setting, preferably at an investment firm.
  • Proficient in PowerBI development, including semantic model relationships, database queries (preferably Snowflake), writing measures in DAX, and advanced visualization techniques.
  • A foundational understanding of credit funds and portfolio management.
  • Demonstrated project management skills and excellent organizational skills; ability to effectively prioritize multiple projects with diligent attention to detail.
  • Self-starter with initiative. Ability to work unsupervised.
  • Strong interpersonal skills with capacity to interact with outside parties and personnel across all levels of the firm.
  • Collaborate daily with team members to brainstorm approaches, challenge assumptions, and improve deliverables through shared iteration.
  • Strong ability to think outside the box to develop solutions to Frequent / Challenging / Unique ad-hoc requests to deliver the desired information, often starting from a blank slate.

DESIRED BACKGROUND/EDUCATION

  • A bachelor’s degree in data analytics, accounting, finance, or a related field is preferred.
  • Strong analytical background utilizing PowerBI to summarize large datasets.
  • Proficient writing code in Python, DAX, and SQL
  • A keen interest in technology and its application in finance, with the capacity to propose and implement process improvements.
  • CPA or equivalent qualification a plus.
  • Work toward or an interest in pursuing a CFA charter a plus.

CAREER DEVELOPMENT OPPORTUNITIES

  • The role offers ample opportunity for growth into senior finance or analytical roles within the firm.
  • Utilize PowerBI to create and refine interactive dashboards, presenting asset and portfolio data effectively.
  • Ongoing exposure to advanced data analysis tools and methodologies will be provided.
  • Potential to contribute to firm-wide strategic initiatives and influence data governance practices.

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $100,000 - $120,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.
