jobs.title: Senior Associate, Product Content – Global Private Wealth
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

We’re looking for a Senior Associate, Product Content to help define how our products are understood across HarbourVest’s Global Private Wealth platform.

In this role, you’ll work closely with investment professionals, product specialists, and marketing partners to turn complex strategies into clear, compelling messaging for wealth audiences. You’ll help shape how our products are positioned in the market and ensure that story comes through consistently across materials.

You’ll also create timely content tied to market developments, investment insights, and fundraising priorities so our messaging stays relevant, current, and useful in real conversations with clients.

The ideal candidate is someone who is:

  • Thinks clearly and can turn complex ideas into simple, compelling narratives
  • Understands how strong messaging supports fundraising and advisor engagement
  • Works effectively with investment teams and asks the right questions
  • Produces accurate, high-quality work with strong attention to detail
  • Stays curious about markets and identifies opportunities to create relevant content
  • Manages multiple priorities and keeps work moving forward

What you will do:

  • Own and continuously improve product narratives, including value proposition, positioning, and differentiation
  • Translate investment philosophy, process, and portfolio construction into messaging that is clear and relevant for wealth audiences
  • Build and maintain core product materials, including presentations, pitchbooks, and evergreen content
  • Create timely content such as market commentary, investment perspectives, and product-related thought leadership
  • Ensure product messaging remains consistent and up to date across regions, channels, and client-facing materials
  • Partner with investment professionals and product specialists to gather inputs and refine product positioning
  • Work with Product Marketing to ensure messaging is accurately reflected in sales enablement deliverables and client materials
  • Collaborate with Client Marketing to incorporate product narratives into campaigns and events
  • Incorporate feedback from Investor Relations and client-facing teams to strengthen product positioning over time
  • Manage content development end-to-end, coordinating across investment, legal, compliance, and marketing teams
  • Ensure all materials meet high standards for accuracy, consistency, and regulatory requirements
  • Act as a steward of product narrative integrity, ensuring consistency and differentiation across all product-related communications.
  • And other responsibilities as required

What you bring:

  • Experience supporting private wealth clients, platforms, or distribution channels strongly preferred
  • Strong understanding of private markets strategies, investment processes, and portfolio construction
  • Proven experience developing product value propositions and client-facing narratives
  • Strong writing, storytelling, and presentation development skills
  • Comfort partnering directly with investment professionals and synthesizing complex information
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

Education Preferred

Bachelor of Science (B.S) or equivalent experience

Experience

5-8 years experience in private markets product marketing, investment communications, product specialist roles, or related functions within asset management or alternatives.

$120,000.00 - $130,000.00

jobs.title: Quantitative Product Specialist
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

We are seeking a dynamic and client-focused Quantitative Product Specialist for the Quantitative Investment Science (QIS) team. This role involves connecting with clients and prospects, driving adoption of proprietary quantitative tools, and collaborating with internal teams to provide data-driven insights that improve investment decisions. The ideal candidate presents ideas clearly, possesses strong quantitative knowledge, and enjoys delivering impactful solutions.

What you will do

Strengthen client and prospect engagement:

  • Collaborate with Investor Relations and Client Service to actively involve clients and prospects using QIS tools, presenting them as key resources for manager selection and portfolio construction.
  • Host product demos and facilitate deeper conversations with investment experts to highlight QIS as a strategic differentiator.
  • Contribute to lead generation and define success metrics for product engagement and team interactions.
  • Build educational materials, commentary, standard explanatory content, talking points, and presentations that simplify complex quantitative concepts for diverse internal and external audiences.

Drive adoption of QIS tools and solutions:

  • Join forces with investment teams to apply detailed, data-driven insights in decision-making processes, boosting efficiency and reinforcing strategies for stronger outcomes.
  • Build stakeholder buy‑in through tailored engagements, impactful presentations, and demonstrations that showcase how QIS solutions elevate investment processes.

Lead Marketing Strategy & Execution:

  • Work with marketing teams to develop compelling product materials, presentations, insights, and thought leadership content showcasing QIS capabilities.
  • Support investor communications by translating quantitative concepts into clear, client-friendly messaging.

Product Development and Competitor Intelligence:

  • Partner with Product Management to refine product roadmap based on market trends and investor feedback.
  • Capture competitive intelligence and find opportunities for product differentiation.
  • Collaborate with marketing to host and attend industry events, broadening QIS brand exposure.
  • Operations & Reporting:
  • Define, measure and communicate important metrics to key internal collaborators on QIS platform engagement.
  • Improve cross-functional partnerships and integrate QIS efforts into firm-wide processes.
  • Plug into current workflows and systems to build repeatable, scalable processes that improve efficiency and impact.

What you bring

  • Analytical approach with strong attention to detail.
  • Ability to balance technical rigor with creative marketing approaches.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to build relationships and convey complex concepts clearly.
  • Collaborative teammate with strong interpersonal skills.
  • Strategic problem solver with a client-centric approach.
  • Passion for defining new processes, systems, and guidelines to scale support practices.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, or related field preferred; CFA or CAIA designation a plus.
  • 5+ years of experience in institutional relationship management or business development, private equity, asset management, or related financial services role with a quantitative focus preferred.
  • Demonstrated ability to communicate complex analytics to non-technical audiences; strong presentation and writing skills.
  • Experience in marketing strategy and content development within financial services is a plus.
  • Strong proficiency in data analysis tools (Excel, Python, R) and financial modeling a plus.

#LI-Hybrid

Base Salary Range

$125,000.00 - $175,000.00

jobs.title: Quantitative Researcher, Private Equity Secondaries
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

As a member of the Quantitative Investment Sciences (QIS) team, this quantitative researcher will join a highly diverse and growing group of expert researchers, data engineers and software developers who are pioneering the development and application of quantitative models and tools to private market investment decision making and portfolio management. This motivated individual will serve as a dedicated researcher for HarbourVest’s Private Equity Secondary strategy team, generating quantitative insights to inform investment diligence and asset selection, portfolio construction, and support client engagements and fundraising. Our team's projects harness large proprietary private market datasets and statistical models to enhance a traditionally fundamental research-based investment process.

The ideal candidate is someone with:

  • Passion for financial markets and investing
  • Innovative, pragmatic approach to quantitative investment research
  • Entrepreneurial initiative
  • Excels at clearly and effectively communicating insights from quantitative analysis to technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Strives in a collegial and collaborative team-oriented environment
  • Willing to work in a position with uneven and high priority project work

What you will do:

Quantitative modeling and analysis of private equity markets and secondary investment opportunities (80%)

  • Take a lead role in applying models to characterize market risk/return relationships and return drivers, and to evaluate investment opportunities in the PE secondaries market.
  • Accountable for communicating results and actionable insights to the investment team.
  • Lead a strategic research and development plan with investment team leadership.
  • Seek to incorporate new models and techniques (AI/ML) to enhance and scale the investment process.

Support fundraising and ad-hoc quantitative analysis requests (20%)

  • Support client-facing teams and publishing insightful research.

What you bring:

  • Experience in quantitative equity is required.
  • Prior private markets experience is not required.
  • Demonstrate rigorous statistical analysis and experience analyzing large, complex datasets.
  • Strong programming skills, preferably in Python (including numerical, statistical modeling and visualization libraries) and SQL.
  • Prefer prior independent research experience (academic thesis or industry research)

Education:

  • Bachelor of Arts (B.A) or Bachelor of Science (B.S.) required
  • Prefer master's degree or Ph.D. in a technical field

Experience:

  • 3+ years of experience in a quantitative finance role

#LI-Hybrid

Base Salary Range

$180,000.00 - $210,000.00

jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

As a member of the Quantitative Investment Sciences (QIS) team, this quantitative researcher will work with a team of experienced researchers to develop quantitative modeling and analysis for management of private equity evergreen portfolios. This motivated individual will be dedicated to supporting and collaborating with HarbourVest’s Evergreen fund team on pipeline monitoring, liquidity management, portfolio construction, and stress testing while generating quantitative insights for client engagements and fundraising. Projects will involve leveraging large proprietary private market datasets and statistical models to systematize and enhance portfolio management decision making for these sophisticated fund structures.

This is an opportunity to join a highly diverse and growing team passionate about pioneering the application of quantitative research, ML/AI and data science to private markets investing and risk management.

The ideal candidate is someone with:

  • Passion for financial markets and investing, quantitative research with complex datasets, and demonstrated intellectual curiosity.
  • Innovative and entrepreneurial attitude. Comfortable taking initiative.
  • Excels at clearly and effectively communicating quantitative insights.
  • Strives in a collegial and collaborative team-oriented environment.
  • Results and detail oriented.
  • Willing to work in a position with uneven and high priority project work.

What you will do:

Conducting quantitative modeling and analysis to support management of evergreen private equity portfolios (80%)

  • You will play a lead role in leveraging proprietary private markets datasets and models to characterize market risk/return relationships, explore liquidity and market stress events, and develop strategies to optimize liquidity management and fund performance.
  • Accountable for communicating analysis results and actionable insights to the Evergreen fund team.
  • Support and drive adoption and integration of quantitative models to enhance portfolio construction and management processes.

Responding to ad-hoc quantitative analysis requests (20%)

  • Supporting client facing teams in conducting ad-hoc analysis and responding to client requests.

What you bring:

  • Experience in the financial services industry in a quantitative role is required; however, prior private markets experience is not required.
  • Demonstrate rigorous statistical analysis and experience analyzing large datasets.
  • Strong Python programming skills (including numerical, statistical modeling and visualization libraries) with SQL experience is required.
  • Prior experience in Monte-Carlo simulation methods is preferred
  • Ability to learn and enhance an existing code base is required
  • Prefer prior independent research experience in academia or industry

Education:

  • Bachelor of Arts (B.A) or Bachelor of Science (B.S.) required
  • Prefer master's degree or Ph.D. in a technical field

Experience:

  • 3+ years of experience in a quantitative finance role

#LI-Hybrid

Base Salary Range

$210,000.00 - $280,000.00

jobs.title: M&A Associate Director
jobs.description:
Job Title: M&A Associate Director

Contract Type: Permanent, full-time

Location: London, St Pauls

Department: Investment Office

What the Hiring Manager says….

“As an Associate Director with an M&A background in the Investment office, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring our portfolio companies have the right processes, governance and teams in place to originate and execute acquisitions. You will push and challenge these companies, help with origination where possible, and monitor progress to ensure sufficient focus on M&A at the company level. Your extensive network across Europe will be highly relevant in this role.”

Summary of our opportunity

You will be responsible for supporting portfolio companies in their inorganic growth strategies. This role involves ensuring that these companies have efficient origination processes and teams to identify and execute high-value acquisition opportunities. You will work closely with senior management and investment teams. Impact will be measured through value driven for the various stakeholders.

The position will focus on portfolio companies from Europe and Mid-Market Funds with a current portfolio of ~50 companies. The position will be based on London, reporting to the Head of the Investment Office.

Primary responsibilities for this role include (but are not limited to):

Strategic Leadership

  • Ensure portfolio companies have the right processes and teams in place for M&A activities.
  • Push and challenge companies to optimize their origination and execution strategies.

Transaction Support

  • Assist in the origination of acquisition opportunities where possible.
  • Monitor and report on the progress of M&A activities at the company level.

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including investors, advisors, and portfolio company management teams.
  • Leverage your network across Europe to identify and support acquisition opportunities.

Key Requirements/ Qualifications

You will have demonstrable experience of: 

  • Extensive experience in M&A with private equity funds but more importantly Corporates.
  • Proven track record of successful deal origination and execution.
  • Strong financial acumen and analytical skills.
  • Excellent negotiation and communication skills.
  • Ability to lead cross-functional teams and manage complex projects.
  • Nationality and fluency in two or three languages, English ideally combined with French, German, or Spanish.

Personal Attributes

Personal attributes that will make you successful in this role include but are not limited to: 

  • Ability to push things forward and drive progress
  • Good project manager given the multitude of projects across the portfolio
  • Proactive approach to problem-solving.
  • Strong leadership and interpersonal skills
  • Collaborative and team-oriented.

Our Global Benefits Framework is centred around these 3 key attributes:

  • Wellbeing - Supporting your mental, physical and financial wellbeing as well as your lifestyle needs.
  • Inclusivity – Fit for purpose and competitive, no matter who or where you are.
  • Longevity – Long Term and sustainable in design.

Inclusion is one of ICG’s key values and we are committed to creating an equitable and inclusive workplace for everyone. By welcoming different perspectives, we strengthen ICG’s performance, enrich decision-making and contribute to better outcomes for our clients, colleagues, and the markets we serve. Accordingly, our recruitment processes provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees, regardless of age, ethnicity, disability, sex, gender, sexual orientation, religion, or any other characteristic protected by local law.

We are equally committed to delivering an inclusive and accessible candidate experience. If you require information in an alternative format, additional time for assessments, an adjusted interview schedule, or any other support during the recruitment process, please let us know.

jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes:
Company-provided healthcare

A competitive annual leave allowance

Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements

Generous pension provisions

jobs.title: Market Consultant US Ins & SA
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Role:

The Market Consultant will be responsible for building long-standing positive relationships with intermediary clients that increase long-term holdings of Invesco products and services. Serving as the face of Invesco to clients, you will be an integral part of fostering the client journey.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Identifies, develops, and deepens partnership opportunities with Centers of Influence ("COI"), including all levels of client distribution, field investment personnel, and key internal partners at top insurance relationships.

  • Develops and implements sales and retention strategies focused on enhancing Invesco's penetration of the insurance market and growing customer loyalty within the territory.

  • Credibly represents and positions Invesco's investment strategies, including differentiating the firm's strategies from competitor strategies.

  • Partners with strategic accounts, marketing, and client service associates to ensure alignment around business strategy, capitalize on opportunities, and anticipate and overcome challenges.

  • Conducts effective group and individual practice management and Capital Market presentations designed to educate and support insurance COIs and their efforts to better serve their clients and prospects.

  • Develops and continually updates and monitors a current COI list that reflects Invesco's best opportunities based on a quantitative and qualitative firm scoring model for insurance partners.

  • Records client activity to accurately memorialize all interactions and ensure successful allocation of time and resources.

  • Deepens relationship with insurance COIs to extend the Invesco brand to the annuity marketplace and ultimately scale the firm's message across retail broker dealers.

  • Builds and implements strategic travel rotation to cover smaller boutique firms across the country and clients in geographically less desirable locations.

  • Partners with the Division Sales Manager to develop and implement a territory sales plan which is aligned with the overarching division plan.

Requirements of the Role:

  • 5+ years of sales experience with a significant portion of those years focused on specifically marketing insurance products as a sales representative.

  • Series 7 & 63 required

  • CIMA, CFP, CFA and/or CAIA designation preferred

  • Undergraduate Degree in business or other related field and/or equivalent work experience

  • A consistent track record of developing and completing a strategic business plan, including effective client segmentation, prioritization, internal network relationship building, and the effective deployment of internal partners.

  • A strong history of identifying and gaining an audience with key investment decision-makers and influencers.

  • A deep understanding of the competitive landscape, including all insurance VA, FIA, structured products, and how to access Invesco investment and index capability within these offerings.

  • Consultative, client first approach, delivering solutions, not products, to meet client needs and get results.

  • Exceptional public speaking capability improving Invesco's partnership opportunities with premier clients.

  • A growth mindset with a business-owner mentality, which is naturally drawn to opportunities, innovation, new value creation, and looks to continuously improve own capabilities.

  • Excellent listening skills with a natural inclination to focus on client needs coupled with strong presentation and interpersonal skills.

The salary range for this position is $65,000 to $70,000/ year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.title: Municipal Research Analyst
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

Job Purpose (Job Summary): 

Credit Analyst will perform fundamental credit analysis on issuers in the municipal bond market.  Primary responsibilities include the development of fundamental credit opinions, assigning internal credit ratings, and establishing credit outlooks for individual municipal borrowers.  The analyst will also be responsible for monitoring specific legislative, regulatory, economic, and fiscal developments related to the municipal market.  Analyst is expected to actively maintain and communicate internal ratings and other risk assessments to the broader investment team.  Coverage responsibilities would ideally reflect the strengths and professional background of the candidate. 

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Analyst will be expected to identify, assume and manage credit risks and work effectively with investment team members
  • Analyze multiple sectors and issuers within the municipal bond market
  • Produce well researched, concise reports for investment professionals
  • Maintain appropriate and required documentation in database supporting internal credit opinions
  • Actively recommend changes to issuer-specific credit ratings and outlooks
  • Communicate effectively with broader investment team
  • Develop relationships with underwriters, sell-side research, credit rating agencies, professionals of municipal bond issuers and industry organizations 
  • Perform special projects, including AI-related projects, as required by the broader credit team

Work Experience / Knowledge:

  • Up to 8 years of related work experience in fixed-income credit analysis
  • Knowledge of municipal credit analysis preferred

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Ability to cooperate in a team environment is critical
  • Excellent analytical skills, with attention to detail and accuracy
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills needed to effectively share knowledge and opinions with the broader investment team
  • Ability to make decisions and recommendations despite incomplete information, sometimes under stressful conditions
  • Open minded, flexible and willing to listen for other people’s opinions
  • Must exhibit appropriate software knowledge of Windows-based applications, with expertise in Excel, PowerPoint, and Word; experience with Bloomberg a plus

Formal Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics or related field
  • MBA and/or CFA designation is preferred

#MTIVZ

The salary range for this position in Downers Grove, IL and New York is $125,000 -$135,000/year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

General Role Profile

  • Develops an understanding of pricing processes through structured training and on‑the‑job experience

  • Performs a range of routine pricing and valuation tasks with increasing autonomy

  • Applies established procedures to identify discrepancies and escalate issues appropriately

  • Recognizes patterns and anomalies within pricing data using defined validation checks

  • Collaborates closely with colleagues and may serve as a resource for new team members

Responsibilities

  • Obtain daily security prices from approved internal and external sources

  • Perform daily variance, exception, and tolerance review checks

  • Use secondary market data sources (e.g., Bloomberg, JPM, LSEG) to verify and validate pricing inputs

  • Assist in maintaining the accuracy of the daily pricing workflow and controls

  • Support improvements to pricing processes and data quality

  • Assist in documenting good‑faith pricing procedures and valuation processes

  • Prepare standard reports and support data requests related to fund audits

  • Address inquiries from Fund Accountants, Fund Accounting Managers, Compliance specialists, auditors, and Investment professionals

  • Contribute to periodic reporting requirements (monthly, quarterly, annual)

  • Review and validate foreign exchange rates and corporate action impacts

  • Support special projects related to pricing, valuation controls, data remediation, or reporting enhancements

  • Begin developing productive relationships with pricing vendors and market data providers

The Experience You Bring

  • Minimum 1 year of experience in finance, accounting, economics, or a related analytical field within the securities or investment industry

  • Solid understanding of accounting and financial principles relevant to securities pricing

  • Proficiency with spreadsheets and financial data applications; advanced Excel skills a plus

  • Familiarity with market data platforms (ICE, Bloomberg, LSEG) preferred

  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills to collaborate effectively across teams

  • Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with the ability to interpret pricing discrepancies and market data

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or related field

  • Progress toward CFA designation is preferred but not required

  • Foundational knowledge of pricing processes, investment products, and market data sources

  • Ability to apply procedures and controls to ensure accurate daily valuation

  • Developing expertise in a specific area of pricing or security type

jobs.title: Client Portfolio Manager - Custom Equity Platform
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Department / Team:

Invesco is a well-established provider of separately managed account solutions through our Custom SMA platform.  These solutions seek to provide investors with customizable and tax-efficient exposure to equity and fixed income asset classes. The rapidly growing Custom Equity SMA platform is focused on offering highly customized long-only and long/short equity portfolios that allow, among other things, a range of tax management techniques and funding options.

About the Role:

The Client Portfolio Manager (CPM) acts as an integral part of the portfolio management team and possesses deep knowledge of capital markets and the team’s investment philosophy and quantitative investment process. The CPM is regarded as a credible investor who acts as the primary interface between our client-facing teams and the investment team. The CPM interacts with the portfolio management team daily to understand past and current portfolio positioning, performance attribution, the rationale for specific holdings and portfolio activity, risk attributes, views of the team/asset class, and has primary responsibility for conveying this information effectively to retail and institutional clients including gatekeepers, research analysts, consultants, and internal colleagues. The CPM will maintain detailed knowledge of the competitive landscape/peer group, stay current on market trends and developments in all relevant distribution channels, and be able to clearly articulate what differentiates our products from the competition. The CPM acts in partnership with rest of the CPM team, the SMA team, marketing, and other relevant partners on business and marketing related projects. The result is a multi-faceted, dynamic, and challenging role that demands highly developed skills in communications, stage presence, relationship building, business acumen, and analytics. We hire highly engaged, confident individuals who are persistently curious, will not settle for average, and continually strive to improve themselves, their work, and their environment.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Grow and retain business by representing the investment team and key investment strategies to clients.
  • Play a lead role in managing new business opportunities by working with the distribution teams to educate advisors, prospects, and investment platform gatekeepers on Custom Equity strategies – this includes participation in the RFP/RFI process and addressing ad hoc prospect questions.
  • Participate in advisor pitches, due diligence meetings, and stand and deliver presentations as needed.
  • Service existing clients via in-person meetings (on- and off-site), conference calls and investment committee meetings, as needed in place of Portfolio Managers.
  • Provide portfolio updates and insights to drive client engagement and loyalty.
  • Understand distribution opportunities in each channel and help shape product strategy with the Senior CPM and the product manager.
  • Participate in the development of advisor-facing content, presentations, and research.
  • This includes monitoring key investment and industry trends, working with research and product development and identifying issues and topics that leverage insights into the marketplace.
  • Manage the ongoing internal and external experience for our products and work with Marketing to support product specific content.
  • Present at Invesco roundtables and industry conferences.
  • Build strong relationships with the Distribution teams supporting their efforts to execute and effective sales strategy.
  • Support Distribution with training, large conferences, meeting support.
  • Assist Sales with custom proposals for significant business opportunities and participate in client calls/meetings as needed.
  • Liaise with the firm’s various marketing and RFP teams to ensure all data and responses regarding a given product are accurate and appropriate.
  • Assist with quarterly investment commentaries and regulatory commentary content.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Undergraduate degree required, preferably in business or related discipline.
  • 5-8 years of investment-related client-facing experience.
  • Proven success engaging and building relationships with wealth management advisors, gatekeepers, and investment due diligence teams.
  • Experience working with advisors across all channels including wirehouses, RIAs, and independent advisory firms strongly preferred.
  • Entrepreneurial spirit. The Custom Equity team is building upon a newer capability and growing quickly, so a successful candidate must have a desire for team success that allows her or him to identify, own, and solve problems.
  • Technical skills, including strong understanding of quantitative investment processes, understanding of financial markets and economics, proficiency with Bloomberg, Microsoft Office, and other software products.
  • Highly developed communication skills, both written and verbal, including the ability to simplify complex topics into understandable and compelling messages.
  • Strong and influence management skills
  • MBA/CFA a plus
  • Series 7 and 63 or 66 required
  • Regular travel required
The salary range for this position in New York City and Seattle is $150,000 - $180,000 / year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.title: Sr ETF Analyst
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The ETF Product Strategy Group within the Americas Product is committed to providing superior client support and actionable investment insights that drive our product line’s strategic agenda for growth. This individual will work closely with others on the team and help develop ETF research and analysis that positions our fixed income and alternatives ETFs in the current macro environment. The Sr. Analyst will work cross-functionally with Product Development, Portfolio Management, Capital Markets, Sales and Marketing to position and grow the product line.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Work alongside analysts and strategists on the team to develop product segment research and analysis on Invesco ETFs, the ETF industry, and broader financial markets, serving as a subject matter expert for the product line

  • Develop and update product materials that position Invesco’s ETFs

  • Project manage and lead research projects as assigned by senior leaders

  • Execute on actions needed to maintain and improve the product line

  • Partner closely with ETF strategists and specialists to support our clients and ensure product integrity, occasionally participating in client meetings

Requirements of the Role:

  • 3-5 years of investment management and/or research experience preferred

  • Skills and professional development in a senior analyst role with a desire to take on more responsibility and accountability

  • Previous experience or solid understanding of fixed-income and alternatives investing strongly preferred

  • ETF and index familiarity and industry experience required

  • Experience with Bloomberg, Morningstar Direct, FactSet, Python a plus

  • Strong research and analytical skills to analyze and interpret various market research reports and data and apply such information to daily activities

  • Strong subject matter acumen and written communication skills

  • Team player with excellent interpersonal skills; maintain effective, cooperative working relationships with all departments and organizational levels

  • Self-directed; must be comfortable with establishing objectives, then working independently to achieve them, seeking the team’s input as needed

  • Undergraduate degree in business, finance, mathematics or related discipline required

  • Series 7 & 63 (already licensed is a plus, can study for these exams after being hired)

  • CFA track or progress towards advanced strongly preferred

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in New York City is $170,000 to $185,000 annually.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Employee

jobs.title: Senior Risk Analyst, Private Credit Investment Risk
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes: 

  • Company-provided healthcare 

  • A competitive annual leave allowance 

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements 

  • Generous pension provisions 

  • Income protection 

  • Health and wellness benefits 

  • Volunteering days 

  • Enhanced parental leave 

  • Life insurance 

Your role:

You will support Invesco’s risk management capabilities by providing transparency to the senior team on all investment related risks for Private Credit (CLO, Broadly Syndicated Loans, Direct Lending, Special Situations/Distressed and other) portfolios. You will play a key role in the development and implementation of best practices to ensure effective management of market, counterparty, and liquidity risk on behalf of Invesco’s clients.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Work with the Global Head of Alternatives Risk and the Risk Director in formulating direction around Investment Risk strategy and objectives in alignment with Invesco’s mission and investment philosophy.

  • Facilitate investment performance and positive client outcomes through the delivery of commercially aware value-added insights along with effective collaboration with the Investment team.

  • Drive delivery of strategic projects related to technology, data, and human capital to ensure Private Credit Investment Risk has the necessary tools and resources to meet stated objectives.

  • Advance the ongoing buildout of data analytics and data libraries to support deep dive analysis across Private Credit portfolios.

  • Identify key risk exposures and performance drivers; define and monitor risk guidelines and develop quantitative analytics to support risk estimation and management.

  • Maintain an active dialogue with the Private Credit investment teams and facilitate discussion between investment team members around risk profiles, changes to risk composition and firmwide risk appetite.

  • Gather necessary data and develop presentation materials for periodic board updates to ensure Invesco leadership is aware of the performance and state of risk across all Private Credit funds.

  • Continually develop and refine Private Credit Investment Risk’s capabilities to understand the drivers of portfolio performance and alpha generation beyond benchmarking.

  • Lead the ongoing review of risk limits and effective challenge process in consultation with cross-functional teams.

  • Ensure the Private Credit Investment Risk strategy continuously reflects current business environment, market conditions, and legal/regulatory requirements.

  • Anticipate new and emerging risks and play a critical role in providing insights and guidance on the positioning of portfolios to be successful across a wide range of market conditions.

  • Provide guidance for Private Credit risk governance policies and procedures to ensure Private Credit risk meets business/regulatory requirements and function as designed/planned.

What you can bring:

  • Proven experience in relevant financial markets, including at an Asset Manager, Hedge Fund, or Investment Bank.

  • Experience with financial or risk modeling, data analysis of large data sets, dashboard/visualization creation, leveraged loan markets and/or credit analysis.

  • Demonstrated track record building and fostering a wide range of stakeholder relationships and needs.

  • Collaborative and motivated by working in close partnership with colleagues as part of Investment Risk team.

  • Bachelor’s Degree in relevant financial field a plus (Finance, Business, Risk Management, Financial Engineering, etc.).

  • CFA, CAIA, or FRM designation(s) a plus.

Disability Confident Scheme: 

Applicants who opt in to the Disability Confident Scheme and meet the criteria for the role will be offered an interview. We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process for all candidates who make an application. By opting-in to this scheme, applicants will be disclosing that they have a disability solely for the purpose of the Disability Confident Scheme.   

The Disability Confident Scheme only guarantees an interview – it does not automatically mean that applicants interviewed will gain employment with Invesco at that time.  

To apply through the Disability Confident Scheme, you’ll need to firstly ensure you have applied for the role via our external careers page. Following this, you’ll need to email us at emea-talentacquisition@invesco.comconfirming your wish to opt-in, alongside your contact details and the title of the role you wish to apply for.  

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you! We want all of our candidates to shine during the application and selection process, so if you need any adjustments to be made, please send an e-mail to emea-talentacquisition@invesco.com. Please include your name, the job you are interested in, and the type of adjustment you need (for example; breaks during your interview, remote interviews, additional time for assessments or other required adjustments)

We promote a working environment that welcomes everyone and creates inclusive teams, celebrates difference and encourages everyone to be themselves at work.

Our commitment to the community and environmental, social and governance investing:
We partner with charitable organisations globally to make an impact in the communities where we live and work. Our people are encouraged to support the charities they feel most passionate about.  We are also committed to environmental, social and governance (ESG) investing. We serve our clients in this space as a trusted partner both on specific responsible investment product strategies as well as part of our commitment to deliver a superior investment experience. 

Recruitment Agencies: 
Invesco has an in-house recruitment team, which focuses on sourcing great candidates directly. Invesco will not accept unsolicited resumes from agency or search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired. When we do use agencies, we have a PSL in place, so please do not contact hiring managers directly.

Regulatory:
This position may fall in-scope of one or multiple regimes/directives.

jobs.title: Senior Manager, Product Research and Insights, APAC (Hong Kong Based)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

About the Role:

Invesco’s Product Research & Insights team in EMEA and APAC is a dynamic group that drives deep analysis of products, markets, and asset flows to uncover insights that sharpen product positioning, strengthen client engagement, inform product development, and unlock growth opportunities across EMEA and APAC.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Serve as the primary subject‑matter expert on Invesco’s product suite, competitor offerings, and local versus regional industry trends across APAC public and private markets

  • Conduct in‑depth product research to identify competitive advantages and develop differentiated value propositions for internal and external stakeholders

  • Lead the analysis of client demand trends, fund flows, and competitor positioning, synthesising insights into clear recommendations for product development, enhancement, or rationalisation

  • Partner closely with the regional product development team to assess Invesco’s product shelf, identify critical gaps, and support the origination and structuring of new products

  • Act as an APAC ambassador for the broader Product Research & Insights (PR&I) team, leading local initiatives, fostering engagement, and contributing to regional decision making through committee participation and presentations

  • Serve as a strategic partner to regional distribution teams, supporting client engagement through high‑quality product positioning and market‑driven insights

  • Contribute to the client‑led product prioritisation process for APAC, ensuring distribution efforts are aligned with areas of highest client demand and strongest product potential

  • Ensure all activities are in line with Invesco’s Conduct principles

Requirement for the Role:

  • University degree, preferably in a discipline related to financial markets; a CFA designation or equivalent professional qualification is highly desirable.

  • Extensive experience (8+ years) in asset management, ideally in roles that include product research, product positioning, product strategy and effective delivery to senior stakeholders and/or clients.

  • Demonstrated commercial judgement, strong intellectual curiosity, and a high level of execution discipline, with a proven ability to influence outcomes, lead initiatives, and manage complex cross‑functional stakeholder relationships.

    Experience in product research and selection, as well as broader product strategy responsibilities, is highly desirable.

  • Deep and broad understanding of the asset management industry, including public and private markets across all major asset classes. Strong expertise in investment vehicles and fund structures (e.g. mutual funds, UCITS, ETFs, SMAs, alternatives), regulatory frameworks, and the functioning of global financial markets.

  • Good knowledge of portfolio construction principles, benchmarks, factor exposures, and risk/return trade‑offs, combined with a practical understanding of how products are positioned, distributed, and evaluated by clients, consultants, and gatekeepers across regions.

    Well‑grounded awareness of market structure, macroeconomic drivers, and industry trends shaping client demand, product innovation, and competitive dynamics.

  • Full professional fluency in English.

  • Strong proficiency with product, competitor, and flow‑analysis tools such as Morningstar Direct, eVestment, Broadridge, Preqin, and Bloomberg; ability to translate data into actionable insights is a distinct advantage.

  • Working knowledge of programming or automation tools (e.g. Python, VBA) is a plus, particularly where applied to research efficiency, data analysis, or insight delivery.

  • Highly analytical and structured thinker with strong attention to detail and the ability to navigate complex information with clarity and rigour

  • Proven ability to influence outcomes, lead initiatives, and effectively manage cross‑functional stakeholders across multiple functions and seniority levels

  • Self‑motivated with a strong sense of ownership; demonstrates assertive follow‑through, sound judgement, and the ability to operate independently with limited supervision

  • Excellent communication, presentation, and writing skills, with a demonstrated ability to distil complex or technical content into clear, concise, and compelling narratives

  • Strong relationship‑building skills, with the confidence and cultural awareness to collaborate effectively across geographies, cultures, teams, and business areas

How to Apply:

  •  Apply for the role directly at Invesco Careers: Careers at Invesco | APAC.

To know more about us:

  • About Invesco: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/home.html

  • About our Culture: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/about-us/our-culture.html

  • About our CR program: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/our-commitments/corporate-responsibility.html

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


At Invesco, our workplace model supports our culture and meets the needs of our clients while providing flexibility our employees value. As a full-time employee, compliance with the workplace policy means working with your direct manager to create a schedule where you will work in your designated office at least four days a week, with one day working outside an Invesco office.  

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable local laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

Personal data provided by job applicants will be used strictly in accordance with our personal data policy and for recruitment purposes only. Candidates not notified within twelve weeks may consider their application unsuccessful. All related information will be kept in our file for future recruitment purposes. When there are vacancies in our subsidiary or associate companies, we may transfer the application to them for consideration of employment. Under the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance, job applicants have a right to request access to, and to request correction of, Personal Data in relation to the application. If job applicants wish to exercise these rights, they should contact the Human Resources department.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

Job Description

Summary:

The Associate will work across multiple disciplines, including Acquisitions and Asset Management, working closely with experienced investment professionals and receive on-the-job training in each of these fields. 

The Associate will provide support in financial modelling and investment analysis of existing assets in the portfolio, and real estate investment opportunities identified by the Australian Real Estate Team. This role will also assist in the preparation of investment recommendations, investment committee papers, asset business plans, and property and fund reporting.

Depending on the individual’s performance, the Associate may be offered a permanent position at the end or during the 22-month program, if they have displayed the required competencies and delivered high quality output. Performance will be assessed throughout the program.

Primary duties and responsibilities:

  • Assisting the Acquisitions team with deal screening and underwriting of investment opportunities across APAC, including cash flow modeling, return sensitivities, verifying assumptions and market research

  • Participate in preparation of Investment Committee materials

  • Assist in due diligence and research market conditions including location assessment, rent comparables, sales comparables and replacement cost analysis

  • Assisting the Asset Management team in day-to-day active management including capex planning, sell-hold analysis, lease approval memos, refinancing scenarios, preparing annual business plans, coordinating quarterly valuations, updating asset performance and regular reporting

  • Review, analyse and comment on investment recommendations made by other members of the team

  • Other duties and projects as assigned by the Line Manager or other key members of the IRE team.

Formal education:

  • Educated to at least degree level, ideally in property, engineering/science, finance, economics or business studies.


Work experience/knowledge/competencies:

  • 1 - 2 years’ professional experience in finance/investment banking, property brokerage, auditing, consulting, or related fields

  • Proficient in Excel and basic financial modelling

  • Familiarity with real estate investment fundamentals, would be advantageous

  • Superior quantitative analytical and numerical ability

  • Excellent problem-solving skills

  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills (oral and written).


Abilities/personal attributes:

  • Highly numerate

  • Strong time management ability, able to prioritize and manage multiple priorities

  • Ability and willingness to work across multiple disciplines

  • Flexible, adaptable, can-do and proactive attitude

  • Highly motivated with a professional manner

  • Committed to high standards with a focus on attention to detail.

jobs.title: Associate Credit Analyst
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Associate Credit Research Analyst will perform fundamental credit research within multiple sectors of the US investment grade corporate bond market, with a specific focus on utilities and industrials sectors.  The ideal candidate will be deeply interested in markets and investing, intellectually curious, performance-oriented, collaborative, commercial, competitive, and highly driven.

The analyst is expected to become the sector expert, balancing in-depth knowledge of an expansive coverage universe with multiple other responsibilities supporting the broader success of the Invesco Fixed Income platform. Key responsibilities include generating well-supported investment recommendations, conducting in-depth fundamental investment analysis, developing fundamental credit opinions, using credit valuation analysis and sector knowledge to inform relative value views, assigning allocation ratings to each credit in the analyst’s coverage universe, tracking and communicating corporate updates across the analyst’s coverage universe and maintaining a constant dialogue with portfolio managers and investment colleagues across the Invesco Fixed Income platform. The analyst will also be responsible for monitoring broader market and sector risks and opportunities and proactively communicating these to the fixed income platform. This role requires an individual with a deep passion for markets and investing, a positive attitude, a team-focused & commercial mentality, extremely high standards of personal excellence & integrity and keen attention to detail. The analyst is expected to actively maintain and communicate proprietary investment ratings, sector views, investment recommendations and other risk assessments.  The analyst will be expected to identify, assume and manage credit risks and work effectively with analyst team members, traders, portfolio managers, client relations teams, other internal colleagues as well as external partners.  The analyst will be expected to contribute to the broader success of the Invesco Fixed Income platform, effectively participate in sector team presentations and be an active participant in client relations and marketing efforts.

Over time, this role offers the opportunity to grow into a trusted sector specialist whose views meaningfully impact portfolio positioning, performance and client outcomes across the US investment grade platform.

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Develop and articulate defensible investment recommendations, fundamental credit ratings and allocation ratings for each issuer in the analyst’s coverage universe

  • Conduct deep, fundamentally driven investment research on companies within the utility and industrial sectors

  • Develop and communicate issuer level, sector level and broader market relative value assessments

  • Produce well researched, concise investment reports for global investment professionals

  • Be the subject matter expert on relevant regulatory developments that may affect coverage universe

  • Recommend changes in holdings of assigned credits and changes in investment recommendations, both at the individual credit and sector levels

  • Proactively identify emerging risks, opportunities and mispricing's within coverage universe and surface insights to portfolio managers

  • Communicate effectively with investment teams and investment team partners

  • Contribute to sector team presentations and other platform-wide events

  • Make due diligence visits and attend industry conferences as appropriate

  • Ensure all activities are consistent with Invesco’s conduct principles and assigned training is completed on a timely basis

  • Participate in client relations and marketing efforts

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Deep passion for financial markets and investing

  • Financial & investment analysis skills including financial statement analysis and credit valuation analysis

  • Decision making skills and high self-belief / conviction in investment recommendations

  • Growth mindset - demonstrated intellectual curiosity, desire to learn and commitment to continuous improvement as an investor

  • Effective communication, written & interpersonal skills

  • Collaborative, commercial and team-focused mentality

  • Extremely high personal standards and integrity

  • Motivated, competitive self-starter with ability to work independently

Formal Education:

  • Bachelor’s Degree

Work experience:

  • 2 – 5 years of buyside or sellside experience or experience in another finance and investing-related capacity

License / Registration / Certification:

  • CFA or progress towards CFA preferred

#MTIVZ

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.title: ETF Capital Markets Specialist
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes:

  • Company-provided healthcare

  • A competitive annual leave allowance

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements

  • Generous pension provisions

  • Income protection

  • Health and wellness benefits

  • Volunteering days

  • Enhanced parental leave

  • Life insurance

Your Role:

The ETF Capital Markets Specialist is responsible for managing and optimizing the primary and secondary market ecosystem for UCITS ETFs to ensure consistent, high‑quality liquidity and efficient trading across a wide diversified range of exposures throughout all market conditions. Acting as the central link between portfolio management, authorised participants, market makers, exchanges, clients, and internal stakeholders, the role delivers execution expertise, liquidity oversight, and market intelligence. It involves advising on optimal trading strategies, analysing flows and investor behaviour, monitoring market structure and regulatory developments, and translating investment objectives into operationally efficient experience.

What you will be doing:

  • Own the primary market ecosystem by coordinating creations/redemptions, managing new and existing AP relationships, and ensuring efficient ETF liquidity across all market conditions.

  • Optimize secondary‑market quality through continuous monitoring of spreads, depth, and trading behaviour, working closely with market makers to maintain tight and orderly markets.

  • Provide execution intelligence by advising clients on optimal trading strategies, liquidity windows, and execution modalities

  • Assist clients across all channels in achieving efficient execution when trading Invesco ETFs. Provide education and Pre Trade Analytics on ETF liquidity and effectively communicate specific trading strategies and associated costs.

  • Serve as the technical ETF expert for internal teams and clients, explaining structure, liquidity mechanics, and market microstructure in a clear, commercially relevant way.

  • Act as the bridge between portfolio management and the market, translating investment intent into operationally efficient ETF trading and liquidity outcomes.

  • Build and leverage deep product and market expertise to position UCITS ETFs as flexible portfolio and trading solutions across multiple use cases, including ETFs versus other delta‑one exposures, securities finance (create to lend) and more advanced implementations such as structured products and options‑based strategies.

  • Develop and maintain internal and external reports, scorecards and associated capital markets databases. Coordinate execution of key initiatives and projects within the ETF Capital Markets platform across functional groups

  • Write ETF focused market insight pieces and trading research for both internal and external audiences. Collect, analyse and extract meaningful ETF trading data in an effort to produce relevant content and drive inflows.

  • Analyse ETF flow dynamics to interpret investor behaviour, identify liquidity drivers, and translate market activity into actionable insights for sales, PMs, and clients.

What you will bring:

  • Proven experience in the ETF industry or Front Office Buyside equivalent.

  • Relationship building skills to build new and enhance existing relationships with the ETF broker dealer community

  • Understanding of financial markets, investment concepts and trading

  • Analytical skills to analyse and interpret various market data and apply such information to their day to day activities

  • Understanding of ETF structure and mechanics of creation & redemption. Ability to understand and articulate complex investment concepts and strategies

  • Organizational skills and detail orientation. Ability to conduct complex analysis and present data in a meaningful way

  • Ability to work in fast paced dynamic environment, drive forward key initiatives and manage workflow on simultaneous projects

  • Self-directed; must be comfortable with establishing objectives and then working independently to achieve them, seeking managers input as needed

  • Understanding of the ETF trading ecosystem including knowledge of key players and drivers of success in the ETF business

  • Creative and self-motivated individual who works collaboratively in a high performance, team environment

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent preferred

  • Problem solving skills necessary to identify trends and root issues and to recommend solutions that support the company’s business objectives

  • Team player with strong interpersonal skills

  • Some knowledge of SQL, Java, and VBA required to maintain databases and broader ETF Capital Markets platform

  • Proficiency in Bloomberg is a plus

  • Additional languages a plus

  • IMC Qualified a plus

Disability Confident Scheme :

Applicants who opt in to the Disability Confident Scheme and meet the ‘minimum criteria’ for the role will be offered an interview. We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process for all candidates who make an application. By opting-in to this scheme, applicants will be disclosing that they have a disability solely for the purpose of the Disability Confident Scheme.

The Disability Confident Scheme only guarantees an interview – it does not automatically mean that applicants interviewed will gain employment with Invesco at that time.

To apply through the Disability Confident Scheme, you’ll need to firstly ensure you have applied for the role via our external careers page. Following this, you’ll need to email us at EMEA-TalentAcquisition@invesco.com confirming your wish to opt-in, alongside your contact details and the title of the role you wish to apply for.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you! We want all of our candidates to shine during the application and selection process, so if you need any adjustments to be made, please send an e-mail to emea-talentacquisition@invesco.com. Please include your name, the job you are interested in, and the type of adjustment you need (for example; breaks during your interview, remote interviews, additional time for assessments or other required adjustments)

We promote a working environment that welcomes everyone and creates inclusive teams, celebrates difference and encourages everyone to be themselves at work.

Our commitment to the community and environmental, social and governance investing:
We partner with charitable organisations globally to make an impact in the communities where we live and work. Our people are encouraged to support the charities they feel most passionate about.  We are also committed to environmental, social and governance (ESG) investing. We serve our clients in this space as a trusted partner both on specific responsible investment product strategies as well as part of our commitment to deliver a superior investment experience. 

Recruitment Agencies: 
Invesco has an in-house recruitment team, which focuses on sourcing great candidates directly. Invesco will not accept unsolicited resumes from agency or search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired. When we do use agencies, we have a PSL in place, so please do not contact hiring managers directly.

Regulatory:
This position may fall in-scope of one or multiple regimes/directives.
