jobs.title: Senior Director, Credit Asset Management (Los Angeles Office)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Senior Director, Credit Asset Management will be responsible for multiple diverse portfolios of commercial real estate loans originated and held by Invesco’s growing commercial real estate lending business line. The role will include oversight of third-party asset managers and leadership of loan modifications, complex workouts and resolutions.

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Manage third party asset management team comprising 15 dedicated employees of Bellwether Asset Management.  Establish standards, responsibilities and expectations.  Monitor team performance and oversee work product to ensure consistency with Invesco standards.

  • Train third party asset management team and ensure accountability to Invesco standards.

  • Actively evaluate performance of individual asset managers and recommend hires, promotions, performance improvement plans, and terminations.

  • Ensure completion of third party scope of work within specified deadlines.  Evaluate additional scope of work as necessary. Negotiate third party contracts.

  • Lead communication and delegation of responsibilities between Invesco in-house credit team and third party asset management team.

  • Proactively monitor portfolio level performance, with a focus on Watchlist, sub-performing and non-performing loans.

  • Review third party work product and assign ad hoc tasks.

  • Oversee all loan asset management matters including payoffs, prepayments, lease reviews/approvals, SNDAs, reserves, escrows, draw requests, cash management, easements, financial reporting, post-closing items, waiver, modifications, valuation process and borrower requests.

  •   Lead the execution of loss-minimizing recovery strategies such as note sales, workouts, restructures, and/or foreclosure.

  • Collaborate with internal loan originators and accounting team, as well as third-party servicer(s), capital markets team, lenders and legal counsel to drive optimal outcomes for Watchlist, sub-performing and non-performing loans.

  • Lead risk rating process, identify problem / watchlist loans, develop monitoring plans, and recommendations.

  • Lead monthly and quarterly loan valuations processes by managing external and internal valuations professionals and external loan asset managers with valuation oversight responsibilities.  Proactively communicate with Portfolio Management team.

  • Identify and implement process efficiencies, best practices and/or standard operating procedures.

Work Experience / Knowledge:

  • 15+ years of commercial loan asset management or underwriting experience preferred.

  • Construction loan, mortgage workout and senior/sub (ie mezzanine) experience is strongly preferred.

  • Strong management skills with attention to detail and ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.

  • Ability to manage and collaborate with multiple external groups (third party asset manager, third party servicer) and internal groups (portfolio management, valuations, underwriting, origination)

  • Demonstrated expertise in workouts and special situations.

  • Highly proficient user of Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word.

  • Proficient quantitative analysis, research, and problem-solving capabilities.

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Self-motivated and team oriented, with strong time management skills to prioritize deadlines.

  • Collaborative approach.

  • Excellent analytical, verbal and written communication skills. Ability to distill concepts and present results.

  • Confident individual ability to provide checks and balances within internal and external groups

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.

Formal Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance or related field.

  • MBA strongly preferred.

Working Conditions:

  • 5 days in-office in Century City, California.

  • 25% travel

#MTIVZ

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in Los Angeles is $180,000 to $250,000 annually.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

About the Role :

Support asset management activities for Invesco Real Estate in Korea, ensuring strong investment performance and timely delivery of development projects within budget and approved specifications. Collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders to drive asset enhancement, sustainability, and process improvements.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Manage asset performance initiatives (leasing, valuation, development).
  • Assist in partner and advisor relationship management.
  • Support debt financing, reporting, and refinancing processes.
  • Support to prepare annual business plans, budgets, and leasing strategies.
  • Update investment models and cash flow forecasts quarterly.
  • Conduct financial analyses and performance reporting.
  • Assist in internal valuations and review external reports.
  • Support acquisition due diligence and asset-level transactions.
  • Coordinate development and asset enhancement projects.

Requirement for the Role: 

  • Minimum 1-year experience in Korea’s real estate industry.
  • Exposure to development and asset enhancement projects.
  • Familiarity with asset management of income-producing and development assets.
  • Basic financial modeling and valuation skills (Excel, Argus, Estate Master).
  • Strong interpersonal, analytical, and communication skills in Korean and English.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure;

jobs.title: Client Portfolio Manager - Fixed Income
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Invesco Fixed Income (IFI) platform is built on a foundation of deep, proprietary research, robust macroeconomic views and a global network of investment professionals who bring regional expertise. IFI’s robust capabilities span taxable and municipal product offerings including multi-sector as well as sector specific solutions. Our platform’s strength lies in the consistent application of top-down relative value and economic views alongside proprietary fundamental research. We seek to deliver outperformance in a variety of markets within an established risk framework, moderating our risk as conditions and views warrant. The consistency of our process is predicated on stable teams who collaborate across sectors and regions. Ultimately, our client-first culture and disciplined active management empower investors to navigate complex markets with clarity and confidence.

Quick Facts:

●           $536 billion in assets under management as of December 31, 2025

●           175 investment professionals averaging 18 years in the industry and 13 years with Invesco

●           12 primary strategies with a presence in 12 key global markets

Job Purpose:

The IFI Client Portfolio Manager (CPM) acts as an integral part of the portfolio management team and possesses deep knowledge of capital markets and the team’s investment philosophy and process across specified strategies. The CPM is regarded as a credible investment professionals who acts as the primary interface between our client-facing resources and the investment team. The CPM interacts with the portfolio management team daily to understand historical and current portfolio positioning, market conditions and portfolio themes. The CPM is able to communicate performance attribution, the rationale for specific holdings and portfolio activity, risk attributes, and views of the team clearly and succinctly to a variety of institutional clients and institutional / retail stakeholders. The CPM will maintain detailed knowledge of the competitive landscape/peer group, stay current on market trends and developments in all relevant distribution channels, and be able to clearly articulate what differentiates our products from the competition. The CPM acts in partnership with the Client Portfolio Analyst and Product Managers on business plans and marketing content across the product suite they represent.

This is a multi-faceted, dynamic, and challenging role that demands outstanding skills in communications, keen attention to detail, a self-starting attitude, the ability to develop and cultivate trust, business acumen, and analytical prowess. Invesco seeks to hire highly engaged, confident individuals, who do not settle for mediocrity, continually strive for excellence with their firm and peers, and who retain a high degree of intellectual curiosity. 

Key Responsibilities / Duties: 

  • Grow and retain business by representing the investment team and key investment strategies to clients. 

  • Service existing clients via in-person meetings (on- and off-site), conference calls and board / investment committee meetings, as needed in place of Portfolio Managers, or in support of them.

  • Provide end-to-end client servicing such that institutional clients’ needs are met with the same high standards they come to expect with Invesco products.

  • Regularly reflect the investment team by communicating existing (and potential) investment strategies to clients. Provide portfolio updates and insights to drive client engagement and loyalty.

  • Drive the development of research, presentations and investment insights for clients. This includes monitoring key investment and industry trends, working with the thought leadership team to identifytopics that resonate with investors. This individual will leverage publications, speaking engagements, public relations and marketing channels to drive key messages into the market.

  • Attend and present at industry and client conferences including Invesco roundtables, industry events, and PR opportunities.

Secondary Responsibilities / Duties: 

  • Play a key role in developing institutional and wealth opportunities by working with the distribution teams to educate consultants, investment platform gatekeepers and prospects on investment grade and high yield investment strategies.

  • Develop and modify collateral including Finals presentations, DDQs, RFPs and due diligence meeting presentations as needed.

  • Build strong relationships with the Distribution teams supporting their efforts to execute an effective sales strategy.

​Skills / Personal Attributes Required:

  • 10+ years of investment-related client-facing experience preferably covering U.S. investment grade and/or high yield strategies.

  • Experience working with investment platform research analysts, field level financial advisors, retirement plan gatekeepers, investment consultants and/or institutional investment committees for corporations, public entities, and/or endowments and foundations.

  • Deep investment expertise including an understanding of portfolio construction, risk drivers, market dynamics, and product-specific nuances.

  • entrepreneurial spirit, with a focus on continuous improvement, willingness to take risks, embrace and lead change, determination to find creative solutions to problems. Strong work ethic and drive to succeed.

  • Technical skills, including strong investment acumen, understanding of financial markets and economics, proficiency with Bloomberg, Microsoft Office, Morningstar and other software products and comfortability adopting AI tools to enhance productivity.

  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.

  • Ability to collaborate and communicate well with others. Strong team player.

  • Expected at least 25-50% travel.

Formal Education:

  • Undergraduate degree required, preferably in business or related discipline.

  • MBA/CFA and/or direct money management experience preferred.

  • Series 7 and 63 required.

jobs.title: AVP,  ETF Capital Markets – Taiwan (Taiwan Based)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

About the Role:

The AVP, Capital Markets will be responsible for supporting and expanding client‑facing capital markets engagement for Taiwan‑focused strategies, working closely with investment, trading, product, and distribution teams. This role will serve as a subject‑matter expert on Taiwan capital markets, acting as a key interface between investors, internal stakeholders, and market participants.

The specialist will support investor education, market insights, execution guidance, and product positioning, with a strong focus on local market structure, regulation, liquidity, and trading dynamics. The role will also contribute to broader APAC capital markets initiatives and data‑driven decision‑making.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and support client and distributor engagement related to Taiwan capital markets, including market structure, liquidity, execution considerations, and regulatory developments.
  • Act as a trusted specialist for investors, articulating investment philosophy, process, performance, and market context for Taiwan‑focused products.
  • Provide market updates, capital markets insights, and execution guidance to internal stakeholders and external clients.
  • Partner closely with Portfolio Management, Trading, Operations, Legal/Compliance, Product, and Technology teams to support capital markets initiatives.
  • Produce quantitative analysis, market research, and trading analytics to support client discussions and internal decision‑making.
  • Collect, analyze, and interpret trading, flow, and liquidity data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities in the Taiwan market.
  • Contribute to capital markets strategy reporting, including development of tools, dashboards, and visualization to enhance understanding of investor behavior and market dynamics.
  • Support pre‑trade and post‑trade analytics, execution analysis, and reporting aligned with best‑execution objectives.
  • Write Taiwan capital markets insights, commentary, and research for internal and external audiences.
  • Collaborate with product development and research teams on new product launches and enhancements, ensuring alignment with local market requirements.

Experience & Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent with 5+ years of relevant experience in capital markets, trading, or investment roles with a strong focus on Taiwan markets.
  • Deep understanding of Taiwan capital markets, including market structure, trading mechanics, liquidity drivers, and investor behavior.
  • Strong knowledge of multi‑asset classes, including equities, fixed income, derivatives (futures, options, swaps), and FX.
  • Solid understanding of regulatory frameworks relevant to Taiwan (e.g., FSC, TWSE, TPEx) and ability to stay current on regulatory developments.
  • Strong quantitative and analytical skills, with experience producing market, trading, and performance analytics.
  • Proficiency in Bloomberg and market data tools.
  • Working knowledge of programming or data tools (e.g., Python, VBA, SQL) and experience with data visualization or automation is highly desirable.
  • Strong communication and relationship‑building skills, with the ability to explain complex investment and market concepts clearly to diverse audiences.
  • Comfortable operating in a fast‑paced, dynamic environment, managing multiple initiatives simultaneously.
  • Self‑directed, proactive, and able to independently drive initiatives from concept to execution.
  • High learning agility and strong problem‑solving skills, with a mindset focused on continuous improvement and business impact.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Private Markets Product – US Wealth, Real Estate
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Senior Associate will join Invesco’s Private Markets Product – US Wealth, Real Estate team, with primary responsibility for developing and delivering clear, compelling marketing and messaging for private real estate strategies designed for the U.S. wealth channel.

While the role is part of the broader Invesco Private Markets platform, the core of the responsibilities will be focused on how private real estate strategies are positioned, articulated, and visually presented to financial advisors and investors. This includes translating complex private fund structures, portfolio construction concepts, and investment strategies into high-quality, accessible materials across presentations, written content, email, and digital formats. The role will also have the opportunity to get involved in other aspects of Product such as product strategy and product development.

The role requires strong editorial judgment, visual sensibility, and a solid understanding of private markets, with the ability to speak intelligently about different private markets asset classes and strategies and clearly position private real estate within a broader private markets allocation and within retail and wealth management investment portfolios more broadly.

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Develop and refine core messaging and positioning for private real estate strategies offered through the wealth channel.

  • Translate complex private real estate investment concepts into clear, advisor- and investor-friendly narratives.

  • Partner with Marketing to ensure high-quality visual presentation and effective storytelling.

  • Create and maintain presentations, commentaries, FAQs, talking points, and digital materials.

  • Articulate how private real estate compares with and complements other private markets asset classes such as private equity and private credit.

  • Collaborate with Investment Teams, Client PMs, Sales, Compliance, Legal, and Operations.

  • Incorporate feedback from Sales and clients into iterative improvements to messaging and materials.

Work Experience / Knowledge:

  • 4-6 years of experience in financial services with a focus on marketing and communications.

  • Familiarity with private markets, private fund structures, or the financial advisor market is preferred.

  • Strong familiarity with private markets, private fund structures, and the financial advisor market is strongly preferred.

  • Experience or exposure to private real estate is a plus but not required.

  • Ability to communicate differences across private markets asset classes.

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong editorial judgment.

  • Strong PowerPoint skills and visual sensibility.

  • Highly organized, proactive, and collaborative.

Formal Education:

  • Undergraduate degree

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in New York City is $130K to $150K annually.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. Job Description

The Role

The MD Global Liquidity Direct Sales will source, develop, and expand direct institutional client relationships across the United States with a primary focus on growing assets in the firm’s money market funds. You will work closely with the Head of Global Liquidity Relationship Management and senior U.S. distribution leaders to align strategic goals and execute on national growth initiatives.

This role partners extensively with portfolio managers, product teams, and the broader U.S. institutional sales organization to deliver cash‑management insights, drive enterprise engagement, and support the firm’s liquidity strategy across corporations, public entities, nonprofits, and other institutional investors.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Drive growth of U.S. money market fund assets through direct engagement with corporate treasurers, finance leaders, institutional investors, and public sector clients.
  • Increase market share and brand visibility within the U.S. institutional liquidity marketplace.
  • Integrate Global Liquidity priorities with the firm’s national institutional distribution strategy.
  • Partner with Global Portfolio Managers to communicate market conditions, client insights, and evolving product needs.
  • Build and maintain senior‑level relationships across treasury, finance, and investment organizations.
  • Represent the firm through client presentations and participation in U.S. industry conferences, treasury events, and client forums.
  • Collaborate with product, legal, marketing, operations, and compliance teams to support effective business execution and client outcomes.
  • Leverage data, analytics, and CRM discipline to prioritize high‑value opportunities, ensure accurate activity tracking, and support future growth.

Requirements of the role:

  • 10+ years of direct sales experience in the U.S. institutional market, preferably within cash management or liquidity solutions.
  • Deep understanding of money market funds, institutional distribution, and corporate treasury needs within the U.S. investment landscape.
  • Proven track record of raising and retaining institutional assets and consistently meeting or exceeding sales targets.
  • Strong knowledge of pricing, liquidity vehicles, operational workflows, and relationship management in a regulated environment.
  • Ability to anticipate and articulate market trends, regulatory developments, and product changes impacting liquidity management.
  • Strategic, commercially focused mindset with experience operating effectively in a large, global organization.
  • Exceptional communication, presentation, and relationship‑building skills, including engagement with C‑suite and senior treasury leaders.
  • Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred; FINRA registrations required (7, 63) and relevant certifications (e.g., CTP, CFA, CFP) a plus.

The salary range for this position is $160,000 to $185,000/ year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. Job Description

About the Team:

To build solutions that better automate tasks within the Investment Risk organization. The Investment Risk team operates independently to provide objective oversight to senior executives, to portfolio management teams, and to external parties such as Fund boards.  Investment Risk identifies, measures, and manages a wide array of investment-related risks across all portfolios using advanced analytics and qualitative methods.  Investment Risk focuses on portfolio construction to raise awareness of unintended risks that may detract from strategy objectives.

About the Role: This Quantitative Engineer role sits within the Strategy & Execution team, which serves an important function within the Investment Risk group.  Strategy & Execution drives change management, manages projects with enterprise leaders, and builds software to help strengthen the impact of the Investment Risk team.  This role will help redefine the standard of automated solutions used to support the team’s mission, ensuring seamless risk monitoring and alignment with organizational frameworks and objectives.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Degree in a quantitative subject such as Statistics, Economics, Financial Engineering, Computer Science

  • CFA, FRM/PRM and/or MBA (Finance) are desirable

  • Experience with AI and scripting applications used to enhance efficiency

  • Experience as a team player helping to contribute to technology project deliverables

  • Experience working in cloud-based environments such as AWS is a plus

  • Experience in applying machine learning techniques is a plus

  • Not mandatory, but it would be helpful to have previous experience with vendor software commonly used in asset management: Barra, RiskMetrics, FactSet, Charles River, Aladdin, Bloomberg, Morningstar, Macrobond, Compustat, Worldscope, DataStream

  • Intermediate coding skills with extensive hands-on experience in R, Python, SQL or other languages used to engineer large datasets

  • Good understanding of financial instruments and a familiarity with international financial markets (e.g.- ., Equities, Fixed Income, FX, Derivatives)

  • Fundamental understanding of core machine learning concepts is preferred

  • Effectively operate within a team-oriented and collaborative environment

  • Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships across all levels of an organization and to drive stakeholder consensus

  • Intellectually curious with attention to detail

  • Superior problem-solving capability

  • Highly motivated self-starter with ability to operate autonomously and independently

  • Comfort working at the intersection of business, data, and technology, translating needs into practical solutions

  • Ability to communicate process and automation concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

  • Good time management and able to work under occasional time- pressure

  • Comfortable working within a project management team and managing multiple concurrent deliverables

Job Description

About the Role:

The primary role of Senior Manager/Associate Director is responsible to develop and close new business, sales generation in Hong Kong and Macau for complex institutional and pension accounts.

This will involve developing new leads, preparing RFPs, giving presentations to prospective clients, ensuring client and prospects receive full and efficient service and promotes products managed by our office and to extend to other offices within the Group.

Looking for highly motivated candidate with solid industry connection as well as knowledge, who will be able to identify and source deals and to handle the entire Sales process.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Develop new Institutional & Pension business opportunities in Hong Kong and Macau with key prospects, clients and to liaise with related consultants. 
  • Identify potential clients, to manage preparation of presentations, to follow up with the client contacts from sales of investment services to deal close.
  • Evaluate on detailed analysis such as asset liabilities, data research and analysis, investment risk return profile, etc. in particular for pension and insurance clients.
  • Develop in-depth knowledge of Institutional clients, understand their business needs and to effectively assist them in decision making process.
  • Partner with regional and global investment centers to shape the core offerings (traditional long-only strategies, alternatives, multi-assets, insurance solutions, passively managed instruments & indexing capabilities) to cater for different client needs to maximize growth.
  • Generate ideas and tactics, and to coordinate with RFP team to develop innovative responses for the deals.
  • Contribute ideas for new products which will be suitable to the markets and business segments assigned.
  • Achieve activity statistics for the region.
  • Lead or participate in other ad-hoc projects as assigned.
  • Maintains good relationship with all internal stakeholders whilst to understand and to cater for client needs.
  • Contribution in helping the team to reach the leadership position objective with the right optimal approach to meet the needs of Invesco as well as to the clienteles.
  • Ensure that procedures and compliance requirements are compiled.

Requirement for the Role: 

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent – preferably in Finance or Investments related.
  • Relevant professional licenses of the Asset Management industry.
  • At least 6+ years of total relevant working experience
  • Deep knowledge and experience of Institutional clients in Hong Kong and Macau.
  • Strong experience within Insurance segment is a plus
  • Able to think strategically and out-of-the-box
  • Creative in coming up with new ideas in response to prospects’ needs
  • Experience in servicing clients and dealing with sophisticated institutional clients at a senior level
  • Good time management skills
  • Well organized and results focused
  • Ability to multi-task across strategic and tactical work tasks
  • Accurate, articulate, presentable and showing initiative

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Role:

The Associate Quantitative Analyst, Fixed Income will be the primary developer and maintainer of internal systems critical to the daily management of our successful and growing Fixed Income ETF lineup. The Analyst will work under the direction of ETF Portfolio Management to operate and extend tools for automated trading, performance attribution, corporate action analysis, basket negotiation tools and data quality monitoring, among other duties. Successful candidates will be self-directed, curious and eager to identify and implement opportunities to improve the portfolio management process. Knowledge of Python and SQL is required. The ability to work in a group environment with daily interactions with portfolio managers, information technology, and operational staff is expected. General knowledge of ETFs, currency hedging, fixed income securities, trading strategies, broker relationships and portfolio management functions is a plus, but not required. The position, located in Downers Grove, Illinois, will serve as a single contributor with no immediate direct reports. 

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Develop, support and maintain our proprietary risk, flow and order optimization/monitoring system.
  • Own our In-Kind Order negotiation and capture process, working directly with Brokers, Portfolio Management, Trading and Technology to enhance our capabilities.
  • Develop a full understanding of our investment process; become capable of handling orders and rebalances with minimal supervision.
  • Gain a thorough understanding of the global ETF infrastructure and workflow, which includes Portfolio Management requirements for ETF portfolios managed in Europe, Asia, and the United States.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, physics, mathematics, or electrical engineering, or other scientific discipline preferred.
  • Prior experience with Python and SQL is required. Experience with JavaScript (esp. React/Angular), Rust, C, C++, Java, C# or similar is also valuable.
  • Experience with Polars, Plotly Dash (or equivalent), Flask, and the standard python data science stack.
  • Basic knowledge of git is required. Experience with CI tools (GitHub/BitBucket pipelines, Jenkins) is a plus.
  • Working knowledge of Linear Programming at the undergraduate level is desirable. Experience with libraries such as Gurobi/CPLEX/SCIP is a huge plus.
  • Linux experience, or experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.) is a plus.
  • Fixed Income/Trading experience is desirable but not required.
  • Ability to make quick decisions, establish priorities and concentrate on detailed information under time sensitive conditions.
  • Knowledge of the global fixed income markets, prior trading experience, and the ability to provide market color are preferred skills but not required.
  • Proficiency in any of the following PM systems is considered a plus: Aladdin, Bloomberg, Charles River.

The salary range for this position in Downers Grove is $125,000 to $147,000 annually. The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location.

jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes:

  • Company-provided healthcare

  • A competitive annual leave allowance

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements

  • Generous pension provisions

  • Income protection

  • Health and wellness benefits

  • Volunteering days

  • Enhanced parental leave

Your Role:

To ensure successful delivery of new private markets product launches and product change initiatives with an emphasis on technical, structural, regulatory, tax, customer channels, disclosures and operational items, whilst managing internal governance and key stakeholder relationships.

What you will be doing:

  • To independent project manage private market product initiatives from design and approval through to implementation in conjunction with all key business partners.

  • To contribute across all phases of the Product Development Lifecycle with an emphasis on technical, structural, regulatory, tax, customer channels, disclosures and operational business case inputs.

  • Manage internal governance as required (adhering to the requirements of various committees, boards, product development lifecycle and policies and procedures).

  • To successfully manage any issues and risks relating to each product initiative.

  • To manage expectations and escalate any potential delays, issues and/or risks.

  • Relationship building - Develop close working relationships with key stakeholders both within the business (e.g. Investments, Distribution, Control Functions & Operations) and within key counterparts to resolve barriers to the successful achievement of agreed projects.

  • Help drive the team to achieve its strategic goals and contribute to team strategy.  Contribute to the development Invesco EMEA’s role within our Global Private Markets Product function.

  • To take on significant ad hoc projects as required with respect to technical or product related regulatory change and develop end to end solutions to support business needs.

  • Ensure all activities are in line with Invesco’s Conduct principles

What you will bring:

  • Prior experience within the investment management sector and/or financial services industry within a product or related function
    Prior experience with Private Markets product structuring and/or product management

  • Understanding of fund structures, governance, and regulatory frameworks.

  • In depth knowledge of financial markets with investment experience a plus

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, & PowerPoint

  • Familiar with third party data providers

  • Proven communication and interpersonal skills

  • PRINCE2, Agile or another project management accreditation desirable but not essential

  • Educated to Master’s degree level or equivalent from recognised university and/or CFA would be preferable, ideally with finance, mathematics, engineering or economic qualifications

  • Self-motivated and a desire to succeed with a results-based orientation

  • Process and organisational skills

  • Strategic thinking skills

  • Commercial/ business acumen

  • Written communication and oral presentation skills

  • Numeracy skills

  • Methodical and analytical approach

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Team / Department:
The ETF Product Strategy Group within the Americas Product is committed to providing superior client support and actionable investment insights that drive our product line’s strategic agenda for growth.

About the Role:

This individual will work closely with others on the team and help develop ETF research and analysis that positions our US ETFs in the current macro environment. The Sr. Analyst will work cross-functionally with Product Development, Portfolio Management, Capital Markets, Sales and Marketing to position and grow the product line.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Work alongside analysts and strategists on the team to develop product segment research and analysis on Invesco ETFs, the ETF industry, and broader financial markets serving as a subject matter expert for the product line
  • Develop and update product materials that position Invesco’s ETFs
  • Project manage and lead research projects as assigned by senior leaders
  • Execute on actions needed to maintain and improve product line
  • Partner closely with ETF strategists and specialists to support our clients and ensure product integrity, occasionally participating in client meetings

Requirements of the Role:

  • Undergraduate degree in business, finance, mathematics or related discipline required
  • 3-5 years of investment management and/or research experience preferred
  • Series 7 & 63 (already licensed is a plus, can study for these exams after being hired)
  • CFA track or progress towards advanced strongly preferred
  • Skills and professional development in a senior analyst role with a desire to take on more responsibility and accountability
  • Previous experience or solid understanding of factor, thematic or fixed-income investing strongly preferred
  • ETF and index familiarity and industry experience required
  • Experience with Bloomberg, Morningstar Direct, FactSet, Python a plus
  • Strong research and analytical skills to analyze and interpret various market research reports and data and apply such information to daily activities
  • Strong subject matter acumen and written communication skills
  • Team player with excellent interpersonal skills; maintain effective, cooperative working relationships with all departments and organizational levels
  • Self-directed; must be comfortable with establishing objectives, then working independently to achieve them, seeking the team’s input as needed

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in Downers Grove is $110k to $135k annually.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Job Description

Your Role
The Senior Principal will serve as a highly experienced subject matter expert in Bank Loan and Private Credit markets, providing strategic leadership and technical expertise across large-scale transformation initiatives. This role is a senior individual contributor position requiring deep knowledge of bank loan instruments, market infrastructure, and regulatory frameworks. The Senior Principal will drive operational excellence, process optimization, and best practice adoption while collaborating with internal and external stakeholders to ensure superior service delivery and risk mitigation.

You Will Be Responsible For:

  • Lead and influence large-scale transformation projects within Bank Loan Services, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and industry best practices.

  • Develop and implement Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to monitor operational health and performance.

  • Champion continuous improvement initiatives and automation strategies to enhance efficiency and reduce operational risk.

  • Maintain deep understanding of Private Credit and Bank Loan markets, including CLO structures, trade settlement, and lifecycle management.

  • Act as a senior escalation point for complex issues such as restructurings, corporate actions, and new fund launches.

  • Oversee communications and coordination with trustees, agent banks, custodians, and other third-party providers.

  • Ensure robust internal controls and compliance with regulatory requirements.

  • Conduct process reviews and recommend enhancements to achieve best practice standards.

  • Design and implement solutions for new products in collaboration with FO, MO, technology teams and other stakeholders as necessary.

  • Lead audit processes across multiple fund structures with internal and external auditors.

  • Serve as a trusted advisor and mentor, providing training and guidance to team members and leadership as needed.

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders (Front Office, MO, Global Operations, Risk, Legal, Fund Accounting) and external partners.

  • Represent Bank Loan Services in committees, service meetings, and industry forums.


The Experience You Bring:

  • Minimum 15 years of experience in investment industry with a focus on Private Credit and Bank Loan markets.

  • Proven track record of leading complex projects and transformation initiatives.

  • Extensive knowledge of trustee, agent bank, and custodian operations.

  • Familiarity with Wall Street Office Suite, Everest, and S&P Global industry tools/ apps, and rating agency platforms (S&P, Moody’s).

  • Strong understanding of automation tools and data analytics for process improvement.

  • Exceptional leadership and strategic thinking skills.

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills for engaging stakeholders at all levels.

  • BA/BS – Finance/Accounting preferred

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. Job Description

Your Team

The ETF Product Management group is tasked with the development and execution of category and product specific strategies and tactics designed to better position the Invesco ETF product line in the competitive exchange traded product (ETP) market. 


Your Role

The Senior Fixed Income Product Strategist will leverage his/her capital markets, investment management and product knowledge to deliver market views and product positioning to institutions and financial advisors and will develop timely market outlook and sales ideas to address changing market conditions. This individual, in working with the Head of Fixed Income ETF Product Strategy, as part of the Product Strategy and Research Team, will be an expert in the assigned products and categories, and is responsible for supporting the research and strategy content development for the assigned product suite. The individual will utilize a client-focused approach to help communicate product strategy to effectively position and market assigned products.

You Will Be Responsible For:

  • Drive growth of the Fixed Income ETF product line in partnership with cross-functional teams (sales, marketing, capital markets, product management, portfolio management and product development) 

  • Help build and reinforce a brand for Invesco products by presenting at industry conferences and serving as the “voice” for the Fixed Income ETF product line in media interviews, sponsored events, webcasts, etc.

  • Support efforts to develop and manage insights on the product line through

  • Asset class and product-specific communication

  • Product training and positioning

  • Product-specific competitive analysis

  • Partner with Sales to execute product-specific distribution strategies and participate in client meetings

  • Content development in partnership with Marketing

  • Market and product performance commentaries

  • Monitor macro market conditions to formulate asset class views, and translate them into actionable investment and product positioning ideas for clients

  • Identify new product opportunities as well as assess existing product line to identify opportunities for enhancement

  • Help manage team responsible for Invesco Fixed Income ETF Strategy


The Experience You Bring:

  • 10 years of investment management and/or research experience required

  • Previous experience and solid understanding of fixed-income markets required

  • ETF and/or index familiarity and industry experience required

jobs.title: Associate, Private Equity Advisory
jobs.description:
About Kingfish Group
Kingfish is a different kind of private equity firm. We have pioneered a new approach to private equity investing that places us at the intersection of people, ideas and capital. At the core of our efforts are deep, systematic relationships with proven operating executives. Our firm comprises two core business segments; our private equity investment business and an advisory business that supports a limited set of high-performing PE sponsors as they identify, invest in and grow businesses. Our philosophy is to find the best people and give them the freedom to learn, innovate and outperform. In that spirit, we are structured to ensure that each individual contributes substantially to firm strategy and goals, and there is extensive feedback on performance. We pride ourselves on assembling high-performing teams to pursue inspiring goals.
Position Summary
Our Associates work closely with our private equity firm partners, operating as an extension of their deal teams to evaluate investment opportunities and advance portfolio company initiatives. Specifically, Associates are responsible for (a) Building relationships with industry executives and aligning them to support the team throughout the investment process (b) Synthesizing information and translating it into key insights that support investment decision making, (c) Managing projects to deliver high impact insights and relationships that provide an advantage to the team (d) Leading company-wide initiatives that improve how we do our work and make us a stronger team.
Responsibilities
● Build relationships with industry executives and align them to support the team throughout the investment process
  ○ Guide our research team as they identify industry executives and other primary information relevant for a particular investment opportunity
  ○ Lead calls with industry executives, serving as the primary point of contact and ambassador for the firm
●  Synthesize information and translate into key insights that support investment decision making
  ○  Analyze industries, markets and competitive landscapes to better understand investment opportunities
  ○  Perform primary diligence as needed alongside executive advisors throughout the deal process
  ○  Identify key insights from information and recommendations gathered from conversations with industry executives and research
●   Manage projects to deliver high impact insights and relationships, providing an advantage to the team
  ○  Manage project plans and deal timelines, working closely with senior leaders at our private equity partners to ensure our work is focused on the highest priorities and maximum impact
  ○  Create and deliver high quality presentations and memoranda for our Partners as well as Kingfish leadership (e.g. primary diligence deliverables, executive spec documentation, and Kingfish Investment Committee discussions)
●   Lead company-wide initiatives that improve how we do our work and make us a stronger team.
Qualifications
● Bachelor’s degree with 2-3 years of experience within consulting or financial services
● Relationship Building: High EQ with advanced written/ verbal communication skills, executive presence and an ability to build relationships with stakeholders at all levels
● Intellectual Curiosity & Creativity: thoughtful in asking questions and pursuing answers, excited to learn
● Critical Thinking: breaks down complex problems and solves them in creative ways
● Communication & Information Synthesis: effectively identifies key insights and presents insights
● Project Management: ability to manage multiple concurrent projects with tight timelines
● Proactive & Collaborative: team player that takes initiative to improve the team and the firm

jobs.title: Vice President, Global Opportunities - Healthcare
jobs.description:
Our Company

Oaktree is a leader among global investment managers specializing in alternative investments, with more than $220 billion in assets under management. The firm emphasizes an opportunistic, value-oriented, and risk-controlled approach to investments in credit, equity, and real estate. The firm has more than 1,400 employees and offices in more than 25 cities worldwide.

We are committed to cultivating an environment that is collaborative, curious, inclusive and honors diversity of thought. Providing training and career development opportunities and emphasizing strong support for our local communities through philanthropic initiatives are essential to our culture.

Launched in 1988, Global Opportunities, Oaktree’s flagship opportunistic credit strategy, is currently investing out of its $16 billion Fund XII. It employs an all-weather strategy with a broad mandate, and ability to invest across the capital structure in debt or equity with a focus on finding the most attractive public and private opportunities worldwide. The strategy (previously known as Distressed Opportunities) has deep roots in distressed debt, reorganizations, and restructurings, but it has evolved over the last thirty years to include a wide-ranging set of investment activities targeting a mid-teens and above return. These include providing proprietarily originated capital solutions in structured and risk-controlled ways to borrowers with limited access to funding; buying assets in out-of-favor industries; and creating platforms with superior management teams who seek to exploit attractive undervalued, niche, and/or dislocation-driven opportunities.

For additional information, please visit Oaktree’s website at http://www.oaktreecapital.com/

Responsibilities

Oaktree is seeking a Vice President to work within the Opportunities Funds in either the Los Angeles or New York office. The Opportunities Funds specialize in distressed, value-based investing but have a very broad mandate and can invest across the capital structure and in various asset classes, industries and geographies. The role will focus on healthcare coverage across sub-industries and asset classes within the sector. Healthcare is one of the most active industries across the Opportunities Funds portfolio with a strong track record of leading returns. 

The Vice President will participate in all investment functions and portfolio management activities including the following:

  • Preparing detailed financial modeling;
  • Performing quantitative and qualitative investment analysis; 
  • Developing and enhancing business and industry due diligence across healthcare, including idea generation, sourcing and industry relationship development;
  • Participating in deal structuring, legal documentation and negotiations; and
  • Monitoring the performance of existing portfolio investments.

Qualifications

  • Undergraduate degree with an excellent track record of academic achievement
  • 4+ years of investment experience with a top-tier investment firm (ie opportunistic alternative asset manager, private credit, private equity, credit-oriented hedge fund) or investment bank.
  • Exceptional skills in analyzing and presenting investment opportunities, including: business and industry analysis, company valuation, credit analysis, negotiations and structuring.
  • Strong quantitative capabilities in data analysis, financial modeling and presentation of analysis.

Personal Attributes

  • Demonstrates self-initiative and a strong work ethic;
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills;
  • Strong interpersonal skills and a sense of self awareness;
  • High degree of precision in work / attention to detail;
  • Strong problem-solving skills and business acumen; and
  • Team-oriented with strong integrity and professionalism.

Education

Bachelor’s degree from a top tier undergrad and / or MBA degree from a top tier business school

Base Salary Range

$275,000 - $300,000

In addition to a competitive base salary, you will be eligible to receive discretionary bonus incentives, a comprehensive benefits package and a flexible work arrangement.  The base salary offered will be commensurate with experience and/or qualifications, industry knowledge and expertise, as well as prior training and education.

Equal Opportunity Employment Policy

Oaktree is committed to diversity and to equal opportunity employment. Oaktree does not make employment decisions on the basis of race, creed, color, ethnicity, national origin, citizenship, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, past or present physical or mental disability, HIV status, medical condition as defined by state law (genetic characteristics or cancer), pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions, veteran status, military service, marital status, familial status, genetic information, domestic violence victim status or any other classification protected by applicable federal, state and local laws and ordinances. This policy applies to hiring, placement, internal promotions, training, opportunities for advancement, recruitment advertising, transfers, demotions, layoffs, terminations, recruitment advertising, rates of pay and other forms of compensation and all other terms, conditions and privileges of employment. This policy applies to all Oaktree applicants, employees, clients, and contractors. Staff members wishing to report violations or suspected violations of this policy should contact the head of their department or Human Resources.

Oaktree is committed to diversity and to equal opportunity employment. Oaktree does not make employment decisions on the basis of race, creed, color, ethnicity, national origin, citizenship, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, past or present physical or mental disability, HIV status, medical condition as defined by state law (genetic characteristics or cancer), pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions, veteran status, military service, marital status, familial status, genetic information, domestic violence victim status or any other classification protected by applicable federal, state and local laws and ordinances. The firm emphasizes an opportunistic, value-oriented, and risk-controlled approach to investments in credit, equity, and real estate. The firm has more than 1,400 employees and offices in more than 25 cities worldwide.

jobs.title: Investment Analyst, Real Estate
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Investment Analyst role in the Real Estate group is ideal for individuals passionate about pursuing a career in global real estate. This role will provide exposure to the key areas of our Real Estate portfolio – investments, asset management and portfolio management. The main responsibilities will focus on analyzing potential real estate investment opportunities, executing transactions, tracking business plans and reporting on investment performance.

Who you'll work with

You will be joining our Toronto office, working in a dynamic team-based environment where you’ll collaborate closely with members of the Real Estate team across our global offices and function as an important link with internal departments on Fund-wide initiatives. This is a chance to define and differentiate yourself as an institutional global real estate investor and build strategic relationships within the global market. The Real Estate team comprises nearly 40 investment professionals and invests and manages assets across a geographically diverse portfolio of approximately C$28 billion with assets in North America, South America, Europe and Asia, including Australia.

What you'll do

 Investments and Asset Management

  • Build and maintain detailed financial models to evaluate potential investment opportunities.
  • Conduct market research and competitive analysis to support acquisition and disposition decisions.
  • Assist in the due diligence process and incorporate the findings into a financial model.
  • Facilitate sale processes, including preparing materials, engaging with stakeholders, and ensuring timely execution
  • Contribute to investment memos and presentation materials.
  • Support ongoing asset management initiatives, including monitoring investments, tracking business plans and executing key priorities.

Portfolio Management

  • Prepare data-driven portfolio analytics for key portfolio monitoring and reporting initiatives.
  • Measure investment performance by developing an understanding of OTPP returns, benchmark, and risk frameworks to assess historical and projected performance.
  • Support long-term capital planning by preparing deployment forecasts, as well as monitoring short-term liquidity and deployment activity.
  • Support the valuation process, including tracking valuation results and preparing management reporting.
  • Build relationships with other key functions across OTPP and contribute to Fund-wide initiatives.

What you'll need

  • Undergraduate degree required in business, finance, accounting, or related field.
  • Up to one year of experience, preferably in investment banking, principal investing or investment advisory.
  • Analytical Skills: Excellent quantitative, analytical and financial modeling skills with the ability to perform in-depth quantitative analysis.
  • Real Estate Acumen: Understanding of commercial real estate sectors, operating KPIs and valuations.
  • Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint) required. Proficiency in relational database management, cloud-based data warehousing, and data visualization tools is considered an asset.
  • Communication Skills: Strong written and verbal communication skills; with the ability to tell a story to senior stakeholders using data and relevant visualizations.
  • Organizational Skills: Good organizational and time management skills; ability to balance multiple priorities under demanding timelines.
  • Attention to Detail: Disciplined and diligent approach, with rigorous attention to detail and an ability to identify discrepancies or inconsistencies.
  • Intellectual curiosity, and adaptability in an ever-changing, ambiguous environment, with a proactive attitude.

#LI-SS

What we’re offering

Pay-for-performance with base salary and annual incentive based on individual and enterprise performance. The Base salary for this level is $115,000 in Year 1 and $125,000 in Year 2. Individual compensation and placement of this role in the range is based on different factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to relevant experience, skills, demonstrated competencies, and internal equity as they relate to the role.

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Managing Director, Financial Services, Private Capital
jobs.description:
The opportunity

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board (Ontario Teachers') is a global investor with net assets of ~$279 billion as of December 31, 2025. Ontario Teachers’ is a fully funded defined benefit pension plan, and it invests in a broad array of asset classes to deliver retirement security for 346,000 working members and pensioners. Our Private Capital group deploys ~$60 billion across direct private equity investments and private equity funds globally.

The Managing Director will lead investments end-to-end — from origination through exit — sponsor transactions at Underwriting Committee, take active board roles at portfolio companies, and help build and develop the Financial Services investment team. This is a senior seat for a candidate with a proven private equity track record and deep Financial Services expertise.

Who you'll work with

Reporting to the Senior Managing Director, the role will be part of a deal team comprising analysts, associates, principals, directors, and managing directors, supported by third-party consultants, operating executives, and legal and accounting advisors dedicated to each transaction.

The Financial Services team invests across sub-sectors including Insurance, Wealth & Asset Management, Lending, Professional Services, and Cards & Payments, with particular need to understand the regulatory, AI, and digitization themes reshaping the sector. The role will also potentially partner with colleagues in OTPP's global offices on international investment and / or cross asset class opportunities in Financial Services.

What you'll do

  • Lead deal teams end-to-end, from origination through close and through the life of the investment; serve as the named sponsor at Underwriting Committee and author Underwriting Committee papers.

  • Own deal execution with limited oversight — including pricing, structuring, financing, and final commercial and legal negotiating positions.

  • Take active board leadership roles at portfolio companies; drive value creation agendas with management and sponsor follow-on capital, M&A, and exit decisions.

  • Originate proprietary deal flow through personal networks with CEOs, operating executives, sponsors, bankers, and advisors; represent OTPP Financial Services externally.

  • Set coverage priorities and investment theses for one or more sub-sectors; partner with the SMDs on team strategy and sector positioning.

  • Build, lead, and develop the Financial Services investment team; partner with the SMDs on hiring, performance management, and succession. Hold the team accountable for quality of judgment, execution discipline, and alignment with OTPP's mission, vision, and values.

  • Work in a highly collaborative manner, bringing a complex network of stakeholders along (Global and Sector SMDs, deal teams, Portfolio Solutions / Value Creation, international office leaders, portfolio companies). Strike the appropriate balance of being a confident decision-maker and an inclusive leader.

  • Define value creation playbooks across the Financial Services portfolio and hold portfolio company management accountable for delivery.

  • Exercise independent judgment on deal execution within approved mandates; serve as the named sponsor at Underwriting Committee and hold board-level decision authority at portfolio companies.

  • Provide disciplined thinking, sound judgment, and courage of conviction, with a willingness to take balanced, thesis-driven risk. This includes stepping in and intervening directly and effectively when required.

What you'll need

  • ~15+ years of meaningful work experience with a substantial track record of private equity deal leadership. Supplementary experience in management consulting, investment banking, and/or corporate M&A (preferably corporate development or similar) considered in combination with PE tenure.

  • Track record sponsoring deals at Underwriting / Investment Committee and delivering committed returns across a full investment cycle, including conducting and leading multiple diligence streams (financial, operational, legal, tax, insurance), arranging debt financing, and negotiating documentation.

  • Direct investment track record in Financial Services required; insurance, wealth, or payments expertise particularly valued.

  • Deep knowledge of Financial Services sector dynamics — particularly Insurance, Wealth & Asset Management, Lending, Professional Services, and Cards & Payments — and the emerging regulatory, AI, and digitization themes reshaping them.

  • Active “for profit” board director experience, including committee leadership.

  • Strong academic background, with a financial designation (MBA, CFA, CBV, or CA strongly preferred).

  • Demonstrated leadership across the full investment process, including sourcing, due diligence, debt financing, negotiation, and exit.

  • Strong negotiating skills, with ability to lead commercial terms in acquisitions, mergers, and financings.

  • Ability to balance priorities, work independently, lead teams, and thrive in a high-performance environment.

  • Very strong analytical and quantitative skills to perform and guide in-depth, innovative, critical analysis.

  • Deep understanding of fundamental investment analysis and private equity value creation.

  • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to build constructive relationships with internal and external parties, particularly management teams, boards, and senior advisors.

  • Strong communication skills, both oral and written, including IC paper authorship and presentation.

  • Willingness and ability to travel across North America and globally.

#LI-EO1

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Investment Director, Infrastructure & Natural Resources
jobs.description:
Company: Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board

160 Front Street West, Suite 3200

Toronto, Ontario

M5J 0G4

Job Title: Investment Director, Infrastructure and Natural Resources – Latin America

NOC: 10021 – Banking, Credit, and Other Investment Managers

Wage: Starting at an annual base salary of $195,000-215,000 (37.5 hours per week) - permanent/full-time

Location(s) of work: 160 Front Street West, Suite 3200, Toronto, Ontario, M5J 0G4 Canada

Number of Positions: One (1)

Required Language Skills: English. Portuguese and/or Spanish an asset

The Opportunity

Infrastructure & Natural Resources (INR) is seeking an Investment Director to join our Americas team, with a coverage focus on Latin America. Based in Toronto, this role is particularly well-suited to highly driven professionals with significant direct equity investing and/or investment banking experience in infrastructure.

As an Investment Director, you will play a central role across the investment lifecycle -originating and executing new opportunities, while actively managing portfolio companies within the infrastructure sector. In this capacity, you will serve on boards and partner closely with management teams to shape strategy and drive value creation.

This role provides a platform to further deepen your investment expertise across Latin American markets and sectors, while continuing to build credibility and long-term relationships with management teams, partners, and industry participants.

Who you'll work with

Reporting to the Managing Director and Head of South America Infrastructure coverage, the Investment Director will be a key part of deal and asset management teams comprising investment analysts, investment associates, and investment principals, supported by third-party consultants, operating executives, and legal and accounting professionals dedicated to each particular portfolio company.

The broader INR team includes 70 investment professionals leading the execution of marquee infrastructure transactions and actively managing our portfolio companies. With offices in Toronto, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, INR manages a diverse portfolio of c. C$46.5 billion across the Americas, Europe, and Asia.

Key Responsibilities

The Investment Director will play an integral role in both the evaluation and execution of direct equity transactions, as well as in the ongoing management of existing portfolio companies. A key aspect of the role will also involve overseeing, providing guidance to, and mentoring junior team members.

Additionally, they will:

  • Actively manage INR portfolio companies, including serving on boards and partnering with management teams to shape strategy and drive value creation initiatives

  • Originate, evaluate, and pursue selective investment opportunities, with a particular focus on Latin America

  • Play a central role in all stages of equity investment execution, leading key workstreams, overseeing valuation and financial analysis, managing internal teams and external advisors, and making investment recommendations

  • Identify and diligence key business and investment transaction risks, and determine how best to mitigate them

  • Build and maintain relationships with management teams, directors, partners, lenders, advisors, and broader industry participants

  • Supervise and develop junior staff, providing clear direction and ongoing feedback

  • Represent OTPP at regional industry conferences and portfolio company meetings and events

Required Skills

  • 10+ years of professional experience in direct equity investing or investment banking, with a focus on infrastructure

  • 5+ years of investment coverage experience of Latin America is required

  • In-country experience in Brazil, Chile, and/or Mexico is an asset

  • Willingness and ability to travel frequently (20%+)

  • Strong ability to influence outcomes across a range of stakeholders, including management teams, boards, and partners

  • Sound commercial judgement and ability to form independent views and make decisions with imperfect information in a fast-paced environment

  • Strong financial acumen and quantitative skills, including experience overseeing valuation and financial analysis

  • Proven track record in leading the equity investment process, including due diligence, transaction execution, and debt financing

  • Strong project management skills and ability to manage multiple workstreams and teams effectively under tight timelines

·        Fluency in English required. Portuguese and/or Spanish is an asset

Required Education

·        Undergraduate Degree or Master’s Degree in Economics, Commerce, Finance or Business Administration or a related field.

Benefits

  • Pay for performance environment that offers competitive salary and incentives

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage, including a Health Spending Account

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • Competitive time off

  • Discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

  • 20 vacation days

  • Bonus (Discretionary Pay for Performance)

  • Other Benefits (Employee Assistance Program)

  • Medical Benefits

  • Dental Benefits

  • Disability Benefits

  • Life Insurance Benefits

  • Group Insurance Benefits

  • Pension Benefits

  • Vision Care Benefits

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

At Ontario Teachers', diversity is one of our core strengths. We take pride in ensuring that the people we hire and the culture we create, reflect and embrace diversity of thought, background and experience. Through our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion strategy and our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), we celebrate diversity and foster inclusion through events for colleagues to connect for professional development, networking & mentoring.  We are building an inclusive and equitable workplace where our talent is respected, accepted and empowered to be themselves.  To learn more about our commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, check out Life at Teachers'.

How to Apply

Are you ready to pursue new challenges and take your career to the next level? Apply today!

Please send your resume to the following email address: Job_Application@otpp.com and reference Requisition #6990.

Attention: June Melton

You may be invited to complete a pre-recorded interview as part of the recruitment process. This will take no longer than 10 minutes.

Accommodations are available upon request (peopleandculture@otpp.com) for candidates with a disability taking part in the recruitment process and once hired.
